Question: The value of the integral $\int_{\frac{\pi}{24}}^{\frac{5 \pi}{24}} \frac{\mathrm{dx}}{1+\sqrt[3]{\tan 2 \mathrm{x}}}$ is :
Solution:
$\mathrm{I}=\int_{\frac{\pi}{24}}^{\frac{5 \pi}{24}} \frac{\mathrm{dx}}{1+\sqrt[3]{\tan 2 \mathrm{x}}}$
Apply king
$I=\int_{\frac{\pi}{24}}^{\frac{5 \pi}{24}} \frac{d x}{1+\sqrt[3]{\tan 2\left(\frac{\pi}{4}-x\right)}}$
$$
\begin{equation*}
=\int_{\frac{\pi}{24}}^{\frac{5 \pi}{24}} \frac{d x}{1+\sqrt[3]{\cot 2 x}} \tag{2}
\end{equation*}
$$
$\operatorname{Add}(1)+(2) \Rightarrow 2 \mathrm{I}=\int_{\frac{\pi}{24}}^{\frac{5 \pi}{24}}(1) \mathrm{dx}$
$\mathrm{I}=\frac{1}{2}\left(\frac{\pi}{6}\right)=\frac{\pi}{12}$

Question:
For the given cell
Fe2+(aq) + Ag+(aq) →Fe3+ (aq) + Ag(s)
The standard cell potential of the above reaction is Given :
Ag+ + e– → Ag E0 = xV
Fe2+ + 2e– → Fe E0 = yV
Fe3+ + 3e– → Fe E0 = zV
Solution:
Fe2+(aq)+ Ag+ (aq) → Fe3+ (aq) + Ag(s)

\[\Delta G_3^0 = \Delta G_1^0 + \Delta G_2^0\]
–3F(–z) = –2F(–y) + \[\Delta G_2^0\]
\[\Delta G_2^0 = 3Fz - 2Fy\]
Also \[\Delta G_2^0 = - nFE_{F{e^{ + 2}}/F{e^{ + 2}}}^0\]
\[3Fz - 2Fy = - 1F\left( {E_{F{e^{ + 2}}/F{e^{ + 3}}}^0} \right)\]
\[E_{F{e^{ + 2}}/F{e^{ + 3}}}^0 = 2y - 3z\]
\[E_{Cell}^0\]for reaction will be
\[E_{A{g^ + }/Ag}^0 + E_{F{e^{ + 2}}/F{e^{ + 3}}}^0\]
= x + 2y –3z
Option (b)

Question: The species which does not undergo disproportionation reaction is :
Solution:
\[{\text{ClO}}_4^ - \] → x + {(–2) × 4} = – 1 ⇒ x = +7
Chlorine is in its maximum oxidation state, so disproportionation reaction is not possible in \[{\text{ClO}}_4^ - \].

Question: Pressure of an ideal gas, contained in a closed vessel, is increased by 0.4% when heated by 1°C. Its initial temperature must be :
Solution:
250°C
P ∝ T
\[\begin{gathered}
  \frac{{\Delta P}}{P} = \frac{{\Delta T}}{T} \\
  \frac{{0.4}}{{100}} = \frac{1}{T} \\
\end{gathered} \]
T = 250 K

Question: There are ‘n’ number of identical electric bulbs, each is designed to draw a power p independently from the mains supply. They are now joined in series across the main supply. The total power drawn by the combination is :

An electron of mass 'm' with an initial velocity \[\overrightarrow v  = {v_0}\widehat i\left( {{v_0} > 0} \right)\]enters an electric field \[\overrightarrow E  =  - {E_0}\widehat k\]. If the initial de Broglie wavelength is \[{\lambda _0}\], the value after time t would be :-
Solution:
\[\overrightarrow v = {v_0}\widehat i - \frac{{{E_0}e}}{m}t\widehat k\]
\[\left| {\overrightarrow v } \right| = \sqrt {v_0^2 + \frac{{E_0^2{e^2}{t^2}}}{{{m^2}}}} \]
\[{\lambda _0} = \frac{h}{{m{v_0}}}\]
\[\lambda ' = \frac{h}{{m{v_0}\sqrt {1 + \frac{{E_0^2{e^2}{t^2}}}{{v_0^2{m^2}}}} }}\]
\[\lambda ' = \frac{{{\lambda _0}}}{{\sqrt {1 + \frac{{E_0^2{e^2}{t^2}}}{{v_0^2{m^2}}}} }}\]

Question: Density of 3 M NaCl solution is 1.25 g/mL. The molality of the solution is :
Solution:
3M NaCl, dsol = 1.25 gm/mol
Molality\[ = \frac{{M \times 1000}}{{1000d - M \times {M_W}}} = \frac{{3000}}{{1250 - 175.5}}\]
     = 2.79

Question: Two charges q1 and q2 are separated by a distance of 30 cm. A third charge q3 initially at 'C' as shown in the figure, is moved along the circular path of radius 40 cm from C to D. If the difference in potential energy due to movement of q3 from C to D is given by \[\frac{{{q_3}K}}{{4\pi { \in _0}}},\] the value of K is :
Solution:
Potential at C
\[{V_C} = \frac{{k{q_1}}}{{0.4}} + \frac{{k{q_2}}}{{0.5}}\]
Potential at D
\[{V_D} = \frac{{k{q_1}}}{{0.4}} + \frac{{k{q_2}}}{{0.1}}\]
\[\Delta U = \left( {{V_D} - {V_C}} \right)\left( {{q_3}} \right) = \left( {\frac{{k{q_2}}}{{0.1}} - \frac{{k{q_2}}}{{0.5}}} \right)\left( {{q_3}} \right)\]
\[\Delta U = 8k{q_2}{q_3} = \frac{{8{q_2}{q_3}}}{{4\pi {\varepsilon _0}}}\]

Question: Let us consider an endothermic reaction which is non-spontaneous at the freezing point of water. However, the reaction is spontaneous at boiling point of water. Choose the correct option.
Solution:
Reaction is spontaneous at relatively high temperature and non-spontaneous at low temperature \[\Delta \]G = \[\Delta \]H – T\[\Delta \]S
It is only possible when \[\Delta \]H and \[\Delta \]S both are positive.

Question: Consider the given figure and choose the correct option :
Options: Activation energy of backward reaction is E<sub>1</sub> and product is more stable than reactant
Activation energy of forward reaction is E<sub>1</sub> + E<sub>2</sub> and product is more stable than reactant
Activation energy of forward reaction is E<sub>1</sub> + E<sub>2</sub> and product is less stable than reactant.
Activation energy of both forward and backward reaction is E<sub>1</sub> + E<sub>2</sub> and reactant is more stable than product.
Solution:
Activation energy of forward reaction = E1 + E2
Energy of product > Energy of reactant
Stability
Reactant > Product

Question: CrCl3.xNH3 can exist as a complex. 0.1 molal  aqueous solution of this complex shows a depression in freezing point of 0.558ºC. Assuming 100% ionisation of this complex and coordination number of Cr is 6, the complex will be (Given Kf = 1.86 K kg mol–1)
Solution:
Given : ∆Tf = 0.558°C
     Kf = 1.86\[\frac{{K \times kg}}{{{\text{mol}}}}\]
     0.1 m aq. sol.
     ⇒  ∆Tf = i × kf × m
     ⇒  0.558 = i × 1.86 × 0.1
     ⇒  i = 3

Question: The term independent of x in the expansion of ${\left( {\frac{{\left( {{\text{x}} + 1} \right)}}{{\left( {{{\text{x}}^{2/3}} + 1 - {{\text{x}}^{1/3}}} \right)}} - \frac{{\left( {{\text{x}} + 1} \right)}}{{\left( {{\text{x}} - {{\text{x}}^{1/2}}} \right)}}} \right)^{10}},{\text{x}} > 1$ is :
Solution:
${\left( {\frac{{({\mathbf{x}} + 1)}}{{\left( {{{\mathbf{x}}^{\frac{2}{3}}} + {\mathbf{1}} - {{\mathbf{x}}^{\frac{1}{3}}}} \right)}} - \frac{{({\mathbf{x}} - 1)}}{{\left( {{\mathbf{x}} - {{\mathbf{x}}^{\frac{1}{2}}}} \right)}}} \right)^{10}}$
$ = \left( {\left( {{{\mathbf{x}}^{\frac{1}{3}}} + {\mathbf{1}}} \right)} \right.$$ - {\left( {\frac{{\sqrt x + 1}}{{\sqrt x }}} \right)^{10}}$
$ = {\left( {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}}
  {{{\mathbf{x}}^{\frac{1}{3}}} - \frac{1}{{\sqrt {\mathbf{x}} }}}
\end{array}} \right)^{10}}$
\[{T_{r + 1}} = {\,^{10}}{C_r}\,{(x)^{\frac{{10 - r}}{3}}}{( - 1)^r}{(x)^{ - \frac{r}{2}}}\]
\[\frac{{10 - r}}{3} - \frac{r}{2} = 0\]
(20 – 2r) – 3r = 0
r = 4
⇒ 10C4 (− 1)4 = 210

Question: The oxidation state of chromium in the final product formed in the reaction between KI and acidified $\mathrm{K}_{2} \mathrm{Cr}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{7}$ solution is:
Solution: $\mathrm{K}_{2} \mathrm{Cr}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{7}+\mathrm{KI} \xrightarrow{\mathrm{H}^{+}} \mathrm{I}_{2}+\mathrm{Cr}^{3+}$

Question: A particle is released from height S above the surface of the earth. At certain height its kinetic energy is three times its potential energy. The height from the surface of the earth and the speed of the particle at that instant are respectively.
Solution:
V2 = 0 + 2g (S – x)
V2 = 2g (S – x)
At B, Potential energy = mgx
\[mgx = 3 \times \frac{1}{2}m{v^2}\]
\[gx = \frac{3}{2} \times 2g\left( {S - x} \right)\]
4x = S
\[{\text{x}} = \frac{{\text{S}}}{4}\]
\[ \Rightarrow V = \sqrt {2g \times \frac{{3S}}{4}} = \sqrt {\frac{{3gS}}{2}} \]

Question: A monoatomic gas having \[\gamma  = \frac{5}{3}\] is stored in a thermally insulated container and the gas is suddenly compressed to \[{\left( {\frac{1}{8}} \right)^{th}}\]of its initial volume. The ratio of final pressure and initial pressure is: (γ  is the ratio of specific heats of the gas at constant pressure and at constant volume)
Solution:
${{\text{P}}_{\text{i}}}{\text{V}}_{\text{i}}^\gamma = {{\text{P}}_{\text{f}}}{\text{V}}_{\text{f}}^\gamma $
$\frac{{{{\text{P}}_{\text{f}}}}}{{{{\text{P}}_{\text{i}}}}} = {\left( {\frac{{{{\text{V}}_{\text{i}}}}}{{{{\text{V}}_{\text{f}}}}}} \right)^\gamma } = {\left( 8 \right)^{5/3}}$
$\frac{{{{\text{P}}_{\text{f}}}}}{{{{\text{P}}_{\text{i}}}}} = 32$

Question: Half life of zero order reaction A \[ \to \] product is 1 hour, when initial concentration of reaction is 2.0 mol L<sup>–1</sup>. The time required to decrease concentration of A from 0.50 to 0.25 mol L<sup>–1</sup> is:
Solution:
For zero order reaction
Half life = \[\frac{{{A_o}}}{{2k}}\]
60min = \[\frac{2}{{2k}}\]
\[k = \frac{1}{{60}}\,M/\min \]
Now
\[{A_t} = {A_{\text{o}}} - kt\]
\[t = \frac{{{A_{\text{o}}} - {A_t}}}{k}\]
\[ = \frac{{0.5 - 0.25}}{{1/60}}\]
\[0.25 \times 60\]
t = 15 min

Question:
The reaction A2 + B2 → 2 AB follows the mechanism
\[{{\text{A}}_{\text{2}}}\mathop {\mathop \rightleftharpoons \limits^{{k_1}} }\limits_{{k_{ - 1}}} {\text{A + A}}\] (fast)
\[{\text{A + }}{{\text{B}}_{\text{2}}}\xrightarrow{{{k_2}}}{\text{AB + B}}\] (slow)
\[{\text{A + B}} \to {\text{AB}}\](fast)
The overall order of the reaction is :
Solution:
rate = k2[A][B2] …….(1)
\[\left( {\frac{{{k_1}}}{{{k_{ - 1}}}}} \right) = \left( {\frac{{{{\left[ A \right]}^2}}}{{\left[ {{A_2}} \right]}}} \right)\]
\[ \Rightarrow [A] = \sqrt {\frac{{{k_1}}}{{{k_{ - 1}}}}} \sqrt {\left[ {{A_2}} \right]} \]
Substituting in (1) ; we get
Rate = k2 [A][B2]
\[{k_2}\sqrt {\frac{{{k_1}}}{{{k_{ - 1}}}}} {\left[ {{A_2}} \right]^{\frac{1}{2}}}.\left[ {{B_2}} \right]\]
\[\therefore \] order = \[\left( {\frac{3}{2}} \right) = 1.5\]

gsin 60 - μg cos60\[ = \frac{g}{2}\]

\[\mu = \sqrt 3 - 1\]

Question: When a non-volatile solute is added to the solvent, the vapour pressure of the solvent decreases by 10 mm of Hg. The mole fraction of the solute in the solution is 0.2. What would be the mole fraction of the solvent if decrease in vapour pressure is 20 mm of Hg ?
Solution:
p∘= Δp1/xsolute ​​​=10​/0.2=50 mmHg

Δp2​=20 mmHg

xsolute(new)​=p∘/Δp2​​=20/50​=0.4

xsolvent​=1−xsolute(new)​=1−0.4=0.6

Question: Figure shows a current carrying square loop ABCD of edge length is 'a' lying in a plane. If the resistance of the ABC part is r and that of ADC part is 2r, then the magnitude of the resultant magnetic field at centre of the square loop is
Solution:
$\vec B = {\vec B_{{\text{AB}}}} + {\vec B_{{\text{BC}}}} + {\vec B_{{\text{CD}}}} + {\vec B_{{\text{DA}}}}$
\[\vec B = \left[ {\frac{{ - {\mu _0}(2I/3)}}{{4\pi (a/2)}}\sqrt 2  - \frac{{{\mu _0}(2I/3)}}{{4\pi (a/2)}}\sqrt 2 } \right]\]
\[ + \frac{{{\mu _0}(I/3)}}{{4\pi (a/2)}}\sqrt 2  + \frac{{{\mu _0}(I/3)}}{{4\pi (a/2)}}\sqrt 2 ]\hat k\]
\[\vec B = \left[ {\frac{{ - 2\sqrt 2 {\mu _0}I}}{{3\pi a}} + \frac{{\sqrt 2 {\mu _0}I}}{{3\pi a}}} \right]\hat k\]
\[\vec B = \frac{{ - \sqrt 2 {\mu _0}I}}{{3\pi a}}\hat k\]

During the melting of a slab of ice at 273 K at atmospheric pressure :
Options: Internal energy of ice-water system remains unchanged.
Positive work is done by the ice-water system on the atmosphere.
Internal energy of the ice-water system decreases.
Positive work is done on the ice-water system by the atmosphere.
Solution: Volume decreases during melting of ice so positive work is done on ice water system by atmosphere Heat absorbed by ice water so ∆ Q is positive, work done by ice water system is negative Hence by first law of thermodynamics ∆ U = ∆ Q + ∆ W = Positive So internal energy increases

Heat treatment of muscular pain involves radiation of wavelength of about 900 nm. Which spectral line of H atom is suitable for this ?
Given: Rydberg constant

λ = 900 nm H-atom (Z = 1)
= 9 × 10–5 cm

\[ \Rightarrow \frac{1}{{9 \times {{10}^{ - 5}}cm \times {{10}^5}c{m^{ - 1}}}} = \left( {\frac{1}{{n_1^2}} - \frac{1}{{n_2^2}}} \right)\]
\[ \Rightarrow \frac{1}{{n_1^2}} - \frac{1}{{n_2^2}} = \frac{1}{9}\]

Question:

(A) Covalent radius
(B) Electron affinity
(C) Ionic radius
(D) First ionization energy
Choose the correct answer from the options given below:

Question: Let (a, b) be the point of intersection of the curve x<sup>2</sup> = 2y and the straight line y –2x –6 = 0 in the second quadrant. Then the integral \[I = \int\limits_a^b {\frac{{9{x^2}}}{{1 + {5^x}}}} dx\]is equal to :
Solution:
\[{x^2} = 2y{\text{ }}\& {\text{ }}y = 2x + 6\]
\[{x^2} = 4x + 12\]
\[{x^2} - 4x - 12 = 0 \Rightarrow \left. {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}}
  {x = 6} \\
  {y = 18}
\end{array}} \right|\begin{array}{*{20}{c}}
  {if{\text{ x = }} - 2} \\
  {y = 2}
\end{array}\]
\[\therefore (6,18)\,\,\,\& \,\,\,( - 2,2)\]
Here (6, 18) Rejected because (a, b) lies in 2nd quadrant
\[\therefore a = - 2\,\,\,\& \,\,\,\, = 2\]
\[\therefore I = \int\limits_{ - 2}^2 {\frac{{9{x^2}}}{{1 + {5^x}}}} dx = \int\limits_{ - 2}^2 {\frac{{{{9.5}^x}.{x^2}}}{{1 + {5^x}}}} dx\]
\[\therefore 2I = \int\limits_{ - 2}^2 {9{x^2}dx = 18\int\limits_0^2 {{x^2}dx = 18\left( {\frac{{{x^3}}}{3}} \right)_0^2} } \]
2I = 48
\[\therefore \boxed{I = 24}\]

Question: Designate whether each of the following compounds is aromatic or not aromatic.
Options: e, g aromatic and a, b, c, d, f, h not aromatic
b, e, f, g aromatic and a, c, d, h not aromatic
a, b, c, d aromatic and e, f, g, h not aromatic

Question: A convex lens of focal length 30 cm is placed in contact with a concave lens of focal length 20 cm. An object is placed at 20 cm to the left of this lens system. The distance of the image from the lens in cm is _____
Solution:
Equivalent focal length
$\frac{1}{{\text{f}}} = \frac{1}{{{{\text{f}}_1}}} + \frac{1}{{{{\text{f}}_2}}}$
$ = \frac{1}{{30}} + \frac{1}{{ - 20}} = \frac{{2 - 3}}{{60}} = - \frac{1}{{60}}$
f = − 60 cm
Lens formula
$\frac{1}{{\text{v}}} - \frac{1}{{\text{u}}} = \frac{1}{{\text{f}}}$
$\frac{1}{{\text{v}}} - \frac{1}{{ - 20}} = \frac{1}{{ - 60}}$
${\text{v = - 15 cm}}$

Question: The radius of the smallest circle which touches the parabolas y = x2 + 2 and x = y2 + 2 is
Solution:
The given parabolas are symmetric about the line y = x.

Tangents at A & B must be parallel to y= x line, so slope of the tangents = 1
\[{\left( {\frac{{{\text{dy}}}}{{{\text{dx}}}}} \right)_{\min {\text{A}}}} = 1 = {\left( {\frac{{{\text{dy}}}}{{{\text{dx}}}}} \right)_{\min \,\,{\text{B}}}}\]
For point B, y = x2 + 2
\[\frac{{{\text{dy}}}}{{{\text{dx}}}} = 2{\text{x}} = 1\]
\[{\text{x}} = \frac{1}{2} \Rightarrow {\text{y}} = \frac{9}{4}\]
∴ Point \[{\text{B}} = \left( {\frac{1}{2},\frac{9}{4}} \right)\]⇒ Point \[{\text{A}} = \left( {\frac{9}{4},\frac{1}{2}} \right)\]
\[{\text{AB}} = \sqrt {{{\left( {\frac{1}{2} - \frac{9}{4}} \right)}^2} + {{\left( {\frac{9}{4} - \frac{1}{2}} \right)}^2}} \]
\[ = \sqrt {\frac{{98}}{{16}}} = \frac{{7\sqrt 2 }}{4}\]
Radius\[ = \frac{{7\sqrt 2 }}{8}\]

Question:
Let I be the identity matrix of order 3 × 3 and for the matrix \[A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}}
  \lambda &2&3 \\
  4&5&6 \\
  7&{ - 1}&2
\end{array}} \right],\;|A|\; = - 1.\] Let B be the inverse of the matrix adj(A adj(A2)). Then |(λB + 1)| is equal to _______
Solution:
\[\left| A \right| = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}}
  \lambda &2&3 \\
  4&5&6 \\
  7&{ - 1}&2
\end{array}} \right| = - 1\]
λ (16) – 2 (–34) + 3(–39) = –1
16λ = 48 ⇒ λ = 3
B–1 = adj(A.adj (A2))
Let C = A. adj (A2)
AC = A2 adj(A2) = |A|2.I = I ⇒ C = A–1
Now B–1 = adj(A–1) = B = adj(A)
Now λ B + I ⇒ 3B + I
Let P = 3B + I
P = 3adj (A) + I
AP = 3Aadj(A) + A
AP = 3|A|.I + A
AP = A – 3I
|AP| = |A – 3I|
\[\left| A \right|.\left| P \right| = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}}
  0&2&3 \\
  4&2&6 \\
  7&{ - 1}&{ - 1}
\end{array}} \right| = 38\]
|P| = –38

Question:

Solution:
As it is difficult to predict order using data provided in graph.
For specific time interval 0 - 5 sec, 5 – 10 sec and 10 – 15 sec. order comes to be zero, but graph is not a straight line. Assuming 1st order kinetics
\[K = \frac{1}{t}In\frac{{{A_0}}}{{{A_t}}}\]
\[K = \frac{1}{{10}}In\frac{{40}}{{20}}\]
Time required to reduce to 2.5 g/L
\[K = \frac{1}{t}In\frac{{50}}{{2.5}}\]
\[\frac{1}{{10}}In{\text{ }}2 = \frac{1}{t}In20\]
\[t = \frac{{1.3010 \times 10}}{{0.3010}} = 43.3{\text{ }}sec.\]

Question:
Given three identical bags each containing 10 balls, whose colours are as follows :
RedBlueGreen
Bag I325
Bag II433
Bag III514


A person chooses a bag at random and takes out a ball. If the ball is Red, the probability that it is
from bag I is p and if the balls is Green, the probability that it is from bag III is q, then the value of \[\left( {\frac{1}{p} + \frac{1}{q}} \right)\] is :
Solution:
\[p = P\left( {\frac{{{B_I}}}{R}} \right) = \frac{{\frac{1}{3}\left( {\frac{3}{{10}}} \right)}}{{\frac{1}{3}\left( {\frac{3}{{10}} + \frac{4}{{10}} + \frac{5}{{10}}} \right)}} = \frac{1}{4}\]
\[q = \left( {\frac{{{B_{III}}}}{G}} \right) = \frac{{\frac{1}{3}\left( {\frac{4}{{10}}} \right)}}{{\frac{1}{3}\left( {\frac{5}{{10}} + \frac{3}{{10}} + \frac{4}{{10}}} \right)}} = \frac{1}{3}\]
\[\therefore \frac{1}{p} + \frac{1}{q} = 7\]

Question: If the equation of the hyperbola with foci (4, 2) and (8, 2) is 3x<sup>2</sup>–y<sup>2</sup>– αx + βy + γ = 0, then α + β + γ is equal to ______.
Solution:
Equation of hyperbola is
\[\begin{gathered}
  \frac{{{{\left( {x - 6} \right)}^2}}}{{{a^2}}} - \frac{{{{\left( {y - 2} \right)}^2}}}{{4 - {a^2}}} = 1 \\
   \Rightarrow \;\left( {4 - {a^2}} \right){\left( {x - 6} \right)^2} - {a^2}{\left( {y - 2} \right)^2} = {a^2}\left( {4 - {a^2}} \right) \\
\end{gathered} \]
\[\begin{gathered}
  comparing\;with\;3{x^2} - {y^2} - \alpha x + \beta y + \gamma = 0,\;we\;get \\
  {a^2} = 1\;and\;\alpha = 36,\;\beta = 4\;and\;\gamma = 101 \\
  \therefore \;\alpha + \beta + \gamma = 141 \\
\end{gathered} \]

Question: Let the area of the triangle formed by a straight Line L : x + by + c = 0 with co-ordinate axes be 48 square units. If the perpendicular drawn from the origin to the line L makes an angle of 45° with the positive x-axis, then the value of b<sup>2</sup><sup> </sup>+ c<sup>2</sup> is:
Solution:
\[\frac{x}{{ - c}} + \frac{y}{{ - c/b}} = 1\]

\[\therefore \] area of triangle\[ = \frac{1}{2}\left| {\frac{{{c^2}}}{b}} \right| = 48\]
\[\left| {\frac{{{c^2}}}{b}} \right| = 96\]
\[\because - c = - \frac{c}{b}\]
\[ \Rightarrow b = 1\] \[\therefore {c^2} = 96\]
\[\therefore {b^2} + {c^2} = 97\]

Question: If the domain of the function \[{\text{f}}\left( {\text{x}} \right) = {\log _{\text{e}}}\left( {\frac{{2{\text{x}} - 3}}{{5 + 4{\text{x}}}}} \right) + {\sin ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{4 + 3{\text{x}}}}{{2 - {\text{x}}}}} \right)\] is \[\left[ {\alpha ,\beta } \right)\], then α 2 + 4β is equal to
Solution:
Given function is \[{\text{f}}\left( {\text{x}} \right) = {\log _e}\left( {\frac{{2{\text{x}} - 3}}{{5 + 4{\text{x}}}}} \right) + {\sin ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{4 + 3{\text{x}}}}{{2 - {\text{x}}}}} \right)\]
For domain, the conditions are
\[\frac{{2{\text{x}} - 3}}{{5 + 4{\text{x}}}} > {\text{and}}\left| {\frac{{4 + 3{\text{x}}}}{{2 - {\text{x}}}} \leqslant 1} \right|\]
Now, \[\frac{{2{\text{x}} - 3}}{{5 + 4{\text{x}}}} > 0 \Rightarrow x \in \left( { - \infty , - \frac{5}{4}} \right) \cup \left[ {\frac{3}{2},\infty } \right)\]
And \[ - 1 \leqslant \frac{{4 + 3{\text{x}}}}{{2 - {\text{x}}}} \leqslant 1\]
\[ \Rightarrow \left( { - 1 \leqslant \frac{{4 + 3{\text{x}}}}{{2 - {\text{x}}}}} \right) \cap \left( {\frac{{4 + 3{\text{x}}}}{{2 - {\text{x}}}} \leqslant 1} \right)\]
\[ \Rightarrow \left( {\frac{{6 + 2{\text{x}}}}{{2 - {\text{x}}}}} \right) \cap \left( {\frac{{2 + 4{\text{x}}}}{{2 - {\text{x}}}} \leqslant 0} \right)\]
\[ \Rightarrow \frac{{6 + 2{\text{x}}}}{{2 - {\text{x}}}}.\frac{{2 + 4{\text{x}}}}{{2 - {\text{x}}}} \leqslant 0\]
\[ \Rightarrow {\text{x}} \in \left[ { - 3, - \frac{1}{2}} \right]\]
Hence, we get the domain of f as \[{\text{x}} \in \left[ { - 3, - \frac{5}{4}} \right)\]
This means that \[\alpha = - 3,\,\beta = - \frac{5}{4}\]
Thus, \[{\alpha ^2} + 4\beta = 9 - 5 = 4\]

Question: Let the Mean and Variance of five observations x<sub>1</sub>= 1, x<sub>2</sub> = 3, x<sub>3</sub> = a, x<sub>4</sub> = 7 and x<sub>5</sub> = b, a > b, be 5 and 10 respectively. Then the Variance of the observations n + x<sub>n</sub>, n = 1, 2, …….. 5 is
Solution:
\[\begin{gathered}
  \bar x = \frac{{\sum {{x_i}} }}{n} = \frac{{1 + 3 + a + 7 + b}}{5} = 5 \\
  a + b = 14 \\
\end{gathered} \]
\[\begin{gathered}
  {\sigma ^2} = \frac{{\Sigma x_i^2}}{n} - {\left( {\bar x} \right)^2} \\
   \Rightarrow \;\frac{{{1^2} + {3^2} + {a^2} + {7^2} + {b^2}}}{5} - 25 = 10 \\
\end{gathered} \]
\[\begin{gathered}
  {a^2} + {b^2} = 116 \\
  a > b\;a = 10\;b = 4 \\
  n + {x_n}:\;2,5,13,11,9 \\
\end{gathered} \]
\[\begin{gathered}
  {\sigma ^2} = \frac{{{2^2} + {5^2} + {{13}^2} + {{11}^2} + {9^2}}}{5} - {\left( {\frac{{2 + 5 + 13 + 11 + 9}}{5}} \right)^2} \\
   = 80 - 64 = 16 \\
\end{gathered} \]

Question: If \[\mathop {\lim }\limits_{x \to 0} \frac{{\cos \left( {2x} \right) + a\cos \left( {4x} \right) - b}}{{{x^4}}}\]is finite, then (a+b) is equal to :
Solution:
\[\mathop {\lim }\limits_{x \to 0} \frac{{\cos 2x + a\cos 4x - b}}{{{x^4}}} = finite\]
\[L = \frac{{\left\{ {1 - \frac{{{{\left( {2x} \right)}^2}}}{{2!}} + \frac{{{{\left( {2x} \right)}^4}}}{{4!}}} \right\} + a\left\{ {1 - \frac{{{{\left( {4x} \right)}^2}}}{{2!}} + \frac{{{{\left( {4x} \right)}^4}}}{{4!}}} \right\} - b}}{{{x^4}}}\]
\[L = \frac{{\left( {1 + a - b} \right) - {x^2}\left( {2 + 8a} \right) + {x^4}\left( {\frac{2}{3} + \frac{{32}}{3}a} \right) + {x^6}()......}}{{{x^4}}}\]
\[\therefore 1 + a - b = 0\]and \[2 + 8a = 0 \Rightarrow a = - \frac{1}{4}\]
\[b = a + 1\]
\[ = - \frac{1}{4} + 1 = \frac{3}{4}\]
\[\therefore a + b = - \frac{1}{4} + \frac{3}{4} = \frac{1}{2}\]

Question: Which of the following postulate of Bohr's model of hydrogen atom is not in agreement with quantum mechanical model of an atom ?

When an electron makes a transition from a higher energy stationary state to a lower energy stationary state, then it emits a photon of light
The electron in a H atom's stationary state moves in a circle around the nucleus
Solution: The electron in a H-atom’s stationary state moves in a spherical path.

Question: The sum \[1 + \frac{{1 + 3}}{{2!}} + \frac{{1 + 3 + 5}}{{3!}} + \frac{{1 + 3 + 5 + 7}}{{4!}} + ...\]upto ∞ terms, is equal to
Solution:
\[S = 1 + \frac{{1 + 3}}{{2!}} + \frac{{1 + 3 + 5}}{{3!}} + ...\]
\[ = \sum\limits_{r = 1}^\infty {\frac{{{r^2}}}{{r!}}} \]
\[ = \sum\limits_{r = 1}^\infty {\frac{{\left( {r - 1 + 1} \right)}}{{\left( {r - 1} \right)!}} = \sum\limits_{r = 2}^\infty {\frac{1}{{\left( {r - 2} \right)!}} + \sum\limits_{r = 1}^\infty {\frac{1}{{\left( {r - 1} \right)!}}} } } \]
= 2e

Question: In the following reactions, which one is NOT correct?

Let (1 + x + x<sup>2</sup>)<sup>10</sup> = a<sub>0</sub> + a<sub>1</sub>x + a<sub>2</sub>x<sup>2</sup>+....+ a<sub>20</sub>x<sup>20</sup>. If (a<sub>1</sub> + a<sub>3</sub> + a<sub>5</sub> + ....+ a<sub>19</sub>) – 11a<sub>2</sub> = 121k, then k is equal to_____.
Solution:
(1 + x + x2)10 = a0 + a1x + a2x2 + …. + a20x20
∴ 310 = a0 + a1 + a2 + …. + a20 ….(i)
1 = a0 – a1 + a2 ….. + a20 …(ii)
\[\left( i \right) - \left( {ii} \right) \Rightarrow \;{a_1} + {a_3} + .... + {a_{19}} = \frac{{{3^{10}} - 1}}{2} = 29524\]
Also {1 + x(1 + x)}10 = 1 + 10C1x (1 + x) + 10C2x2(1 + x)2+…..
\[\begin{gathered}
  \therefore \;{a_2} = {\;^{10}}{C_1} + {\;^{10}}{C_2} = 55 \\
  \therefore \;\frac{{\left( {{a_1} + {a_3} + ... + {a_{19}}} \right) - 11{a_2}}}{{121}} = 239 \\
\end{gathered} \]

Question: A molecule with the formula AX<sub>4</sub> Y has all it’s elements from p-block. Element A is rarest, monoatomic, non-radioactive from its group and has the lowest ionization enthalpy value among A, X and Y. Elements X and Y have first and second highest electronegativity values respectively among all the known elements. The shape of the molecule is :
Solution:
Given A is rarest, monoatomic, non-radioactive p-block element and form AX4 Y type of molecule.
∴ It is concluded that it is Xe
It is given the electronegativity of A is less than X & Y
It is given the electronegativity of X & Y is highest and second highest respectively among all element. ∴ X & Y are F & O
∴ Compound is consider as XeOF4 with square pyramidal shape.

Question: Let A be a 3 × 3 real matrix such that A<sup>2</sup>(A – 2I) – 4(A – I) = O, where I and O are the identity and null matrices, respectively. If \[{A^5} = \alpha {A^2} + \beta A + \gamma I,\] where \[\alpha ,\beta \] and \[\gamma \] are real constants, then \[\alpha  + \beta  + \gamma \] is equal to:
Solution:
A3 – 2A2 – 4A + 4I = 0
A3 = 2A2 + 4A – 4I
A4 = 2A3 + 4A2 – 4A
= 2 (2A2 + 4A – 4I) + 4A2 – 4A
A4 = 8A2 + 4A – 8I
A5 = 8A3 + 4A2 – 8A
= 8(2A2 + 4A – 4I) + 4A2 – 8A
A5 = 20A2 + 24A – 32I
\[\therefore \alpha = 20,\beta = 24,\gamma = - 32\]
\[\therefore \alpha + \beta + \gamma = 12\]

Question: If \[y = \cos \left( {\frac{\pi }{3} + {{\cos }^{ - 1}}\frac{x}{2}} \right),\] then\[{\left( {x - y} \right)^2} + 3{y^2}\] is equal to __________.

Let C be the circle of minimum area enclosing the ellipse E : \[\frac{{{x^2}}}{{{a^2}}} + \frac{{{y^2}}}{{{b^2}}} = 1\]with eccentricity \[\frac{1}{2}\]and foci(±2, 0). Let PQR be a variable triangle, whose vertex P is on the circle C and the side QR of length 2a is parallel to the major axis of E and contains the point of intersection of E with the negative y-axis. Then the maximum area of the triangle PQR is :
Solution:
Area of ∆ PQR
\[ = \frac{1}{2}\left( {2a} \right)\left( {a\sin \theta + b} \right)\]
∴ maximum area = a(a + b)
\[ = 4\left( {4 + 2\sqrt 3 } \right) = 8\left( {2 + \sqrt 3 } \right)\]

Question: If the set of all a \[ \in R - \left\{ 1 \right\}\], for which the roots of the equation (1 – a)x<sup>2</sup> + 2(a – 3)x + 9 = 0 are positive is \[\left( { - \infty , - \alpha } \right] \cup \left[ {\beta ,\gamma } \right),\] then \[2\alpha  + \beta  + \gamma \] is equal to _________.
Solution:
Both the roots are positive
\[D \geqslant 0\]
\[4{\left( {a - 3} \right)^2} - 4 \times 9\left( {1 - a} \right) \geqslant 0\]
\[{a^2} - 6a + 9 - 9 + 9a \geqslant 0\]
\[{a^2} + 3a \geqslant 0\]
\[a\left( {a + 3} \right) \geqslant 0\]
\[a \in \left( { - \infty , - 3} \right] \cup \left[ {0,\infty } \right)...........\left( i \right)\]
\[ - \frac{b}{{2a}} > 0\]
\[\frac{{2\left( {a - 3} \right)}}{{2\left( {a - 1} \right)}} > 0\]
\[a \in \left( { - \infty ,1} \right) \cup \left( {3,\infty } \right).......\left( {ii} \right)\]
f(0) = 9 > 0
Equation \[(i) \cap (ii)\]
\[a \in \left( { - \infty , - 3} \right] \cup \left[ {0,1} \right)\]
\[2\alpha + \beta + \gamma - 6 + 0 + 1 = 7\]

Question:
If \[\theta \in \left[ { - \frac{{7\pi }}{6},\frac{{4\pi }}{3}} \right],\] then the number of solutions of
\[\sqrt 3 \cos e{c^2}\theta - 2\left( {\sqrt 3 - 1} \right)\cos ec\theta - 4 = 0,\] is equal to
Solution:
\[\cos ec\theta = \frac{{2\left( {\sqrt 3 - 1} \right) \pm \sqrt {4\left( {3 + 1 - 2\sqrt 3 } \right) + 16\sqrt 3 } }}{{2\sqrt 3 }}\]
\[ = \frac{{2\left( {\sqrt {3 - 1} } \right) \pm \sqrt {16 + 8\sqrt 3 } }}{{2\sqrt 3 }}\]
\[ = \frac{{2\left( {\sqrt 3 - 1} \right) \pm \left( {2 + 2\sqrt 3 } \right)}}{{2\sqrt 3 }}\]
\[\cos ec\theta = 2{\text{ }}or{\text{ }}\frac{{ - 2}}{{\sqrt 3 }}\]
\[\therefore \sin \theta = \frac{1}{2}or\frac{{ - \sqrt 3 }}{2}\]
\[\therefore \] sin\[\theta \] =\[\frac{1}{2}\]has 3 solutions & also sin\[\theta = \frac{{ - \sqrt 3 }}{2}\]
has 3 solutions in \[\left[ {\frac{{ - 7\pi }}{6},\frac{{4\pi }}{3}} \right]\]

Question: \[If\;\mathop {Lim}\limits_{x \to 0} \;{\left( {\frac{{\tan x}}{x}} \right)^{\frac{1}{{{x^2}}}}} = p,\]then $96 \log_{e}p$ is equal to ___
Solution:
\[\begin{gathered}
  P = \mathop {\lim }\limits_{x \to 0} {\left( {\frac{{\tan x}}{x}} \right)^{\frac{1}{{{x^2}}}}} \\
   \Rightarrow \;P = {e^{\mathop {\lim }\limits_{x \to 0} \;\left( {\frac{{\tan x - x}}{{{x^3}}}} \right)}} \\
\end{gathered} \]
\[ = {e^{\mathop {\lim }\limits_{x \to 0} \;\frac{{\left( {x + \frac{{{x^3}}}{3} + \frac{{2{x^5}}}{{15}} + ...... - x} \right)}}{{{x^3}}}}}\]
\[\begin{gathered}
   = {e^{1/3}} \\
  \therefore \;96{\log _e}^p = 96 \times \frac{1}{3} = 32 \\
\end{gathered} \]

Question: Let y = y(x) be the solution of the differential equation \[\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} + 3\left( {{{\tan }^2}x} \right)y + 3y = {\sec ^2}x,\]\[y\left( 0 \right) = \frac{1}{3} + {e^3}.\] Then \[y\left( {\frac{\pi }{4}} \right)\]is equal to
Solution:
\[\begin{gathered}
  \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} + 3\left( {{{\sec }^2}x} \right)y = {\sec ^2}x,y\left( 0 \right) = \frac{1}{3} + {e^3} \\
  If\; = {e^{3\int {{{\sec }^2}xdx} }} = {e^{3\tan x}} \\
\end{gathered} \]
∴ Solution is
\[\begin{gathered}
  {e^{3\tan x}}y = \int {{e^{3\tan x}}{{\sec }^2}} xdx \\
  {e^{3\tan x}}y = \frac{{{e^{3\tan x}}}}{3} + c \\
\end{gathered} \]
\[\begin{gathered}
  \because \;y\left( 0 \right) = \frac{1}{3} + {e^3} \Rightarrow c = {e^3} \\
  \therefore \;y\left( {\frac{\pi }{4}} \right) = \frac{{\frac{{{e^3}}}{3} + {e^3}}}{{{e^3}}} = \frac{4}{3} \\
\end{gathered} \]

Question: If the orthocentre of the triangle formed by the lines y = x + 1, y = 4x – 8 and y = mx + c is at (3, –1), then m – c is :
Solution:
Solve line PQ & QR
\[\begin{gathered}
  {\text{Point}}\;Q\;\left( {\frac{{1 - c}}{{m - 1}},\;\frac{{1 - c}}{{m - 1}} + 1} \right) \\
  {m_{2H}} = \frac{{\frac{{1 - c}}{{m - 1}} + 2}}{{\frac{{1 - c}}{{m - 1}} - 3}} = \frac{{1 - c + 2m - 2}}{{1 - c - 3m + 3}} = \frac{1}{4}\;\;\;\;...\left( 1 \right) \\
\end{gathered} \]
\[\because \;{m_{PH}} = \frac{5}{0} \to \infty \]
⇒ Slope of line QR (m) = 0
Put value of m in equation (1)
\[\frac{{1 - c - 2}}{{1 - c + 3}} = - \frac{1}{4} \Rightarrow c = 0\]
so m – c = 0 Ans.

Question: A line passing through the point \[{\text{P}}\left( {\sqrt 5 ,\sqrt 5 } \right)\]intersects the ellipse \[\frac{{{{\text{x}}^2}}}{{36}} + \frac{{{{\text{y}}^2}}}{{25}}\]= 1 at A and B such that (PA). (PB) is maximum. Then 5(PA<sup>2</sup> + PB<sup>2</sup>) is equal to:
Solution:
Given ellipse is \[\frac{{{{\text{x}}^2}}}{{36}} + \frac{{{{\text{y}}^2}}}{{25}} = 1\]

Any point on line AB can be assumed as
\[Q\left( {\sqrt 5 + r\cos \theta ,\sqrt 5 + r\sin \theta } \right)\]
Putting this in equation of ellipse, we get
\[25{\left( {\sqrt 5 + r\cos \theta } \right)^2} + 36{\left( {\sqrt 5 + r\sin \theta } \right)^2} = 900\]
Simplifying, we get
\[{r^2}\left( {25{{\cos }^2}\theta + 36{{\sin }^2}\theta } \right) + 2\sqrt 5 r\left( {25\cos \theta + 36\sin \theta } \right) - 595 = 0\]
\[\left| {\text{r}} \right| = {\text{PA,PB}}\]
Thus, \[PA.PB{\text{ }} = {\text{ }}\frac{{595}}{{25{{\cos }^2}\theta + 36{{\sin }^2}\theta }} = \frac{{595}}{{25 + 11{{\sin }^2}\theta }}\]
= maximum, if sin2θ = 0
This means line AB must be parallel to x-axis
\[ \Rightarrow {{\text{y}}_{\text{A}}} = {{\text{y}}_{\text{B}}} = \sqrt 5 \]
Putting \[{\text{y}} = \sqrt 5 \]in equation of ellipse, we get
\[\frac{{{{\text{x}}^2}}}{{36}} + \frac{1}{5} = 1 \Rightarrow {{\text{x}}^2} = 36.\frac{4}{5}\]
Hence,
\[{\text{P}}{{\text{A}}^2} + {\text{P}}{{\text{B}}^2} = {\left( {\sqrt 5 - \frac{{12}}{{\sqrt 5 }}} \right)^2} + {\left( {\sqrt 5 + \frac{{12}}{{\sqrt 5 }}} \right)^2}\]
\[ = 2\left( {5 + \frac{{144}}{5}} \right) = \frac{{338}}{5}\]
\[5\left( {{\text{P}}{{\text{A}}^2} + {\text{P}}{{\text{B}}^2}} \right) = 338\]

Question: Let α and β be the roots of \[{{\text{x}}^2} + \sqrt {3}\text{x}   - 16 = 0,\] and γ and δ be the roots of x2 + 3x –1 = 0. If Pn = α n + β n and Qn = γ n + δ n, then \[\frac{{{{\text{P}}_{25}} + \sqrt {3}\text{P}}_{24}}{{2{{\text{P}}_{23}}}} + \frac{{{{\text{Q}}_{25}} - {{\text{Q}}_{23}}}}{{{{\text{Q}}_{{\text{24}}}}}}\] is equal to
Correct order of limiting molar conductivity for cations in water at 298 K is :
Solution:
Limiting Molar Conductivities of Ions :
• \[\mathop {\text{H}}\limits^ \oplus {\text{: 349 }}{\text{.8 Sc}}{{\text{m}}^{\text{2}}}{\text{mo}}{{\text{l}}^{ - 1}}\]
• \[{\text{N}}{{\text{a}}^{\text{ + }}}:50.11{\text{Sc}}{{\text{m}}^2}{\text{mo}}{{\text{l}}^{ - 1}}\]
• \[{{\text{K}}^{\text{ + }}}:73.52{\text{Sc}}{{\text{m}}^{\text{2}}}{\text{mo}}{{\text{l}}^{ - 1}}\]
• \[{\text{C}}{{\text{a}}^{ + 2}}:119{\text{Sc}}{{\text{m}}^{\text{2}}}{\text{mo}}{{\text{l}}^{ - {\text{1}}}}\]
• \[{\text{M}}{{\text{g}}^{ + 2}}:106.12\,{\text{Sc}}{{\text{m}}^{\text{2}}}{\text{mo}}{{\text{l}}^{ - {\text{1}}}}\]
Therefore correct order of limiting molar conductivity of cations will be –
\[\mathop {\text{H}}\limits^ \oplus {\text{ > C}}{{\text{a}}^{{\text{ + 2}}}} > {\text{M}}{{\text{g}}^{{\text{ + 2}}}} > {{\text{K}}^ + } > {\text{N}}{{\text{a}}^ + }\]

Question: Let the domain of the function ƒ(x) = log2 log4 log6 (3 + 4x – x2) be (a, b). If \[\int_0^{b - a} {\left[ {{{\text{x}}^2}} \right]{\text{dx}}} = {\text{p}} - \sqrt {\text{q}} - \sqrt {\text{r}} ,{\text{p}},{\text{q}},{\text{r}} \in \mathbb{N},\,\gcd \left( {{\text{p, q, r}}} \right) = 1\]where [·] is the greatest integer function, then p + q + r is equal to
Solution:
log4log6(3 + 4x – x2) > 0
log6(3 + 4x – x2) > 1
3 + 4x – x2 > 6
x2 – 4x + 3 < 0
(x – 1) (x – 3) < 0
x ∈ (1, 3)
so a = 1 & b = 3
\[ \Rightarrow \int\limits_0^2 {\left[ {{{\text{x}}^2}} \right]} {\text{ dx = ?}}\]
\[{\text{I = }}\int\limits_0^1 {\left[ {{{\text{x}}^2}} \right]} {\text{ dx + }}\int\limits_1^{\sqrt 2 } {\left[ {{{\text{x}}^{\text{2}}}} \right]{\text{dx + }}\int\limits_{\sqrt 2 }^{\sqrt 3 } {\left[ {{{\text{x}}^2}} \right]{\text{dx + }}\int\limits_{\sqrt 3 }^{\sqrt 4 } {\left[ {{{\text{x}}^2}} \right]} {\text{dx}}} } \]
\[ = 0 + \left| {\text{x}} \right|_1^{\sqrt 2 } + 2\left| {\text{x}} \right|_{\sqrt 2 }^{\sqrt 3 } + 3\left| {\text{x}} \right|_{\sqrt 3 }^{\sqrt 4 }\]
\[ = \left( {\sqrt 2 - 1} \right) + 2\left( {\sqrt 3 - \sqrt 2 } \right) + 3\left( {2 - \sqrt 3 } \right)\]
\[ = 5 - \sqrt 2 - \sqrt 3 \Rightarrow {\text{p}} + {\text{q}} + {\text{r}} = 10\]

Question:
0.2 % (w/v) solution of NaOH is measured to have resistivity 870.0 m\[\Omega \] m. The molar conductivity of the solution will be _______×102 mS dm2 mol–1.
(Nearest integer)
Solution:
Given : Concentration of NaOH = 0.2% (w/v)
\[\therefore \] 0.2 g of NaOH in 100 ml of solution.
Molarity of NaOH solution \[ = \frac{{moles{\text{ of solute}}}}{{{V_{ml}}}} \times 1000\]
\[ = \frac{{0.2/40}}{{100}} \times 1000 = \frac{{0.2}}{{40 \times 100}} \times 1000 = \frac{2}{{40}}M\]
Given resistivity of solution = 870 m ohm m
\[ = 870 \times {10^{ - 3}}ohm{\text{ m}}\]
\[ = 870 \times {10^{ - 3}} \times 10{\text{ }}ohm{\text{ dm}}\]
\[ = 870 \times {10^{ - 2}}{\text{ }}ohm{\text{ dm}}\]
\[ = 8.7{\text{ }}ohm{\text{ dm}}\]
Now conductivity
\[K = \frac{1}{\rho } = \frac{1}{{8.7}}oh{m^{ - 1}}d{m^{ - 1}}\]
Now molar conductivity of solution is
\[{\lambda _m} = \frac{K}{M} = \frac{{\frac{1}{{8.7}}}}{{\frac{2}{{40}}}} = \frac{{40}}{{2 \times 8.7}} = 2.29S{\text{ d}}{{\text{m}}^{\text{2}}}mo{l^{ - 1}}\]
\[2.29 \times {10^3}m{\text{ }}S{\operatorname{dm} ^2}mo{l^{ - 1}}\]
\[ = 22.9 \times {10^2}m{\text{ }}S{\operatorname{dm} ^2}mo{l^{ - 1}}\]
\[ = 23 \times {10^2}m{\text{ }}S{\operatorname{dm} ^2}mo{l^{ - 1}}\]

Question: \[4\int\limits_0^1 {\left( {\frac{1}{{\sqrt {3 + {x^2}}  + \sqrt {1 + {x^2}} }}} \right)} dx - 3{\log _e}\left( {\sqrt 3 } \right)\] is equal to :
Solution:
\[4\int\limits_0^1 {\frac{1}{{\sqrt {3 + {x^2}} + \sqrt {1 + {x^2}} }}} dx - 3\ln \sqrt 3 \]
\[ = 4\int\limits_0^1 {\frac{{\sqrt {3 + {x^2}} - \sqrt {1 + {x^2}} }}{{\left( {3 + {x^2}} \right) - \left( {1 - {x^2}} \right)}}} dx - \frac{3}{2}\ln 3\]
\[ = 2\left[ {\left\{ {\frac{x}{2}\sqrt {3 + {x^2}} + \frac{3}{2}\ln \left( {x + \sqrt {3 + {x^2}} } \right)} \right\}_0^1} \right.\]
\[ - \left. {\left\{ {\frac{x}{2}\sqrt {1 + {x^2}} + \frac{1}{2}\ln \left( {x + \sqrt {1 + {x^2}} } \right)} \right\}_0^1} \right] - \frac{3}{2}\ln 3\]
\[ = 2\left[ {\left\{ {\frac{1}{2}\sqrt 4 + \frac{3}{2}\ln \left( {1 + \sqrt 4 } \right)} \right\} - \left\{ {0 + \frac{3}{2}\ln \sqrt 3 } \right\} - \left\{ {\frac{1}{2}\sqrt 2 + \frac{1}{2}\ln \left( {1 + \sqrt 2 } \right)} \right\} + \left\{ {0 + \frac{1}{2}\left( 0 \right)} \right\}} \right] - \frac{3}{2}\ln 3\]
\[ = 2\left[ {1 + \frac{3}{2}\ln 3 - \frac{3}{4}\ln 3 - \frac{1}{{\sqrt 2 }} - \frac{1}{2}\ln \left( {1 + \sqrt 2 } \right)} \right] - \frac{3}{2}\ln 3\]
\[ = 2 + 3\ln 3 - \frac{3}{2}\ln 3 - \sqrt 2 - \ln \left( {1 + \sqrt 2 } \right) - \frac{3}{2}\ln 3\]
\[ = 2 - \sqrt 2 - \ln \left( {1 + \sqrt 2 } \right)\]

Question: If the four distinct points (4, 6), (–1,5), (0,0) and (k, 3k) lie on a circle of radius r, then 10k + r<sup>2</sup> is equal to
Solution:
m1 m2 = – 1 so right angle equation circle is
(x – 4) (x – 0) + (y – 6) (y – 0) = 0
x2 + y2 – 4x – 6y = 0
(k,3k) lies on it so
k2 + 9k2 – 4k – 18k = 0
10k2 – 22k = 0
\[\begin{gathered}
  k = 0,\;\frac{{11}}{5} \\
  k = 0\;is\;not\;possible\;so\;k = \frac{{11}}{5} \\
\end{gathered} \]
\[\begin{gathered}
  also\;r = \sqrt {4 + 9} = \sqrt {13} \\
  so\;10k + {r^2} = 10.\frac{{11}}{5} + {\left( {\sqrt {13} } \right)^2} = 35 \\
\end{gathered} \]

Question: If the domain of the function f(x) = log<sub>7</sub>(1 – log<sub>4</sub>(x<sup>2</sup> – 9x + 18)) is (α, β) ∪ (γ,δ), then α + β + γ + δ is equal to

Let g be a differentiable function such that \[\int\limits_0^x {{\text{g}}\left( {\text{t}} \right)} {\text{ dt}} = {\text{x}} - \int\limits_0^x {{\text{tg}}\left( {\text{t}} \right)} {\text{ dt, x }} \geqslant {\text{ 0}}\] and let y = y(x) satisfy the differential equation \[\frac{{{\text{dy}}}}{{{\text{dx}}}} - {\text{y}}\tan {\text{x}} = 2\left( {x + 1} \right)\sec {\text{x g}}\left({{\text{x}}} \right),{\text{x}} \in \left[ {0,\frac{\pi }{2}} \right)\]If y (0) = 0, then \[{\text{y}}\left( {\frac{\pi }{3}} \right)\]is equal to
Solution:
Diff. w.r.t. x
g(x) = 1 – xg(x)
\[{\text{g}}\left( {\text{x}} \right) = \frac{1}{{1 + {\text{x}}}}\]
So, \[\frac{{{\text{dy}}}}{{{\text{dx}}}} - {\text{y}}\tan x = 2\sec {\text{x}}\]
IF \[ = {{\text{e}}^ - }\int {\tan {\text{dx}}} = {{\text{e}}^{{\text{log cos x}}}} = \cos {\text{x}}\]
solution of D.E.
y cos x \[ = \int {2{\text{dx + c }}} \]
y cos x = 2x + c
y(0) = 0
\[\boxed{{\text{c}} = 0}\]
\[{\text{y}} = \frac{{2{\text{x}}}}{{\cos {\text{x}}}}\]
y = 2x secx
\[\boxed{{\text{y}}\left( {\frac{\pi }{3}} \right) = 2.\frac{\pi }{3}.2 = \frac{{4\pi }}{3}}\]

Question: The shortest distance between the curves y<sup>2</sup> = 8x and x<sup>2</sup> + y<sup>2</sup> + 12y + 35 = 0 is :
Solution:
Equation of normal to parabola
y2 = 8x is y = mx – 4m – 2m3
passes through (0,–6) we get
–6 = –4m – 2m3
⇒ m3 + 2 m – 3 = 0
⇒ (m – 1) (m2 + m + 3) = 0 ⇒ m = –1
P = (am2, –2am) = (2, –4)
∴ Shortest distance = PC – r
\[ = \left( {2\sqrt 2 - 1} \right)\]

Question:
Consider the following chemical equilibrium of the gas phase reaction at a constant temperature :
\[A(g) \rightleftharpoons B(g) + C(g)\]
If p being the total pressure, Kp is the pressure equilibrium constant and \[\alpha \] is the degree of dissociation, then which of the following is true at quilibrium ?
Options: If p value is extremely high compared to \[{K_p},\alpha  \approx 1\]
If kp value is extremely high compared to \[p,\alpha \]becomes much less than unity
Solution:
\[A(g) \rightleftharpoons B(g) + C(g)\]
t = 0: a, 0, 0
t = t:a (1 – \[\alpha \]), a\[\alpha \], a\[\alpha \]
a moles of A(g) taken initially and at time Now moles fraction of A(g), B(g) and C(g) are
\[{X_A} = \frac{{a - a\alpha }}{{a + a\alpha }} = \frac{{1 - \alpha }}{{1 + \alpha }}\]
\[{X_B} = \frac{{a\alpha }}{{a + a\alpha }} = \frac{\alpha }{{1 + \alpha }}\]
\[{X_C} = \frac{{a\alpha }}{{a + a\alpha }} = \frac{\alpha }{{1 + \alpha }}\]
Now if P is total pressure then partial pressure of A(g), B(g) and C(g) are
\[{P_A} = \left( {\frac{{1 - \alpha }}{{1 + \alpha }}} \right)P\]
\[{P_B} = \left( {\frac{\alpha }{{1 + \alpha }}} \right)P\]
\[{P_C} = \left( {\frac{\alpha }{{1 + \alpha }}} \right)P\]
\[{K_P} = \frac{{\left( {\frac{\alpha }{{1 + \alpha }}} \right)P\left( {\frac{\alpha }{{1 + \alpha }}} \right)P}}{{\left( {\frac{{1 - \alpha }}{{1 + \alpha }}} \right)P}}\]
\[{K_P} = \frac{{{\alpha ^2}P}}{{1 - {\alpha ^2}}}\]
As KP is only function of temperature.
So as P\[ \uparrow \alpha \downarrow \]

Question:
Given below are two statements :
Statement (I) : In octahedral complexes, when ∆ o < P high spin complexes are formed. When ∆ o > P low spin complexes are formed.
Statement (II) : In tetrahedral complexes because of ∆ t < P, low spin complexes are rarely formed. In the light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below:
Options: Statement I is correct but Statement II is incorrect.
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is incorrect but Statement II is correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Solution:
In octahedral complex (CN = 6)
If ∆ 0 < P.E. , then high spin complexes are formed
If ∆ 0 > P.E. , then low spin complexes are formed
But in tetrahedral complex (CN = 4)
∆ t < P.E. , then mainly high spin complexes are formed and rarely low spin complexes are formed.

Question:
When 1 g each of compounds AB and AB2 are dissolved in 15 g of water separately, they increased the boiling point of water by 2.7 K and 1.5 K respectively. The atomic mass of A (in amu) is_______×10–1(Nearest integer)
(Given : Molal boiling point elevation constant is 0.5 K kg mol–1)
Solution:
For AB
\[\Delta {T_b} = 2.7K\]
\[2.7 = 1 \times 0.5 \times m\]
\[m = \frac{{27}}{5}\]
Let molar mass of AB = x.
\[So{\text{ }}\frac{{1/x}}{{15}} \times 1000 = \frac{{27}}{5}\]
\[x = 12.34\]
For AB2
\[\Delta {T_b} = 1.5K\]
\[1.5 = 1 \times 0.5 \times m\]
m = 3
Let molar mass of AB2 = y
\[So\frac{{1/y}}{{15}} \times 1000 = 3\]
\[y = \frac{{1000}}{{45}}\]
\[y = 22.22\]
Now let a and b be atomic masses of A and B respectively, then
A + b = 12.34 …. (i)
A + 2b = 22.22 …. (ii)
B = 22.22 – 12.34 = 9.88
Now a = 12.34 – 9.88 = 2.46
= 24.6 × 10–1 = 25 × 10–1

Question: Let \[\vec a = \hat i + 2\hat j + \hat k,\;\vec b = 3\hat i - 3\hat j + 3\hat k,\]\[\vec c = 2\hat i - \hat j + 2\hat k\;and\;\vec d\] be a vector such that \[\vec b \times \vec d = \vec c \times \vec d\;and\;\vec a.\vec d = 4.\] Then \[{\left| {\left( {\vec a \times \vec d} \right)} \right|^2}\]is equal to ______.
Solution:
\[\vec b \times \vec d = \vec c \times \vec d\;and\;\vec a.\vec d = 4\]
\[\begin{gathered}
   \Rightarrow \;\vec d = \lambda \left( {\vec b - \vec c} \right) = \lambda \left( {\hat i - 2\hat j + \hat k} \right) \\
  \because \;\vec a.\vec d = 4 \Rightarrow \lambda = - 2 \\
\end{gathered} \]
\[\begin{gathered}
  Also,\;{\left| {\vec a \times \vec d} \right|^2} + {\left| {\vec a.\vec d} \right|^2} = {\left| {\vec a} \right|^2}{\left| {\vec d} \right|^2} \\
   \Rightarrow \;{\left| {\vec a \times \vec d} \right|^2} = 6 \times 4 \times 6 - 16 = 128 \\
\end{gathered} \]

Let for λ = λ 1, λ 2, λ 2 < λ 1, the shortest distance between the two lines be 3. Then the square of the distance of the point (λ 1, λ 2, 7) from the line L1 is

\[{\text{SD}} = \frac{{\left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}}
  {\lambda - 1}&3&3 \\

\end{array}} \right|}}{{\left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}}
  {\widehat {\text{i}}}&{\widehat {\text{j}}}&{\widehat {\text{k}}} \\
  0&0&1 \\
  0&1&0
\end{array}} \right|}}\]
\[ = \left| {\lambda - 1} \right| = 3\]
\[\lambda = 4, - 2\]
\[{\lambda _1} = 4\]
\[{\lambda _2} = - 2\]
Let foot of perpendicular from
P(4, –2, 7) is Q(1, 2, t + 3)
So (3, –4, 4 – t) . (0, 0, 1) = 0
\[\boxed{{\text{t}} = 4}\]
So Q(1, 2, 7)
PQ2 = 9 + 16
\[\boxed{{\text{P}}{{\text{Q}}^{\text{2}}} = 25}\]

Question: The number of solutions of equation \[\left( {4 - \sqrt 3 } \right)\sin x - 2\sqrt 3 \]\[{\cos ^2}x =  - \frac{4}{{1 + \sqrt 3 }},x \in \left[ { - 2\pi ,\;\frac{{5\pi }}{2}} \right]\] is
Solution:
\[\left( {4 - \sqrt 3 } \right)\sin x - 2\sqrt 3 {\cos ^2}x = \frac{{ - 4}}{{1 + \sqrt 3 }},\;x \in \left[ { - 2\pi ,\;\frac{{5\pi }}{2}} \right]\]
\[ \Rightarrow \;\left( {4 - \sqrt 3 } \right)\sin x - 2\sqrt 3 \left( {1 - {{\sin }^2}x} \right) = 2\left( {1 - \sqrt 3 } \right)\]
\[ \Rightarrow \;2\sqrt 3 {\sin ^2}x + 4\sin x - \sqrt 3 \sin x - 2 = 0\]
\[\begin{gathered}
   \Rightarrow \;\left( {2\sin x - 1} \right)\left( {\sqrt 3 \sin x + 2} \right) = 0 \\
   \Rightarrow \;\sin x = \frac{1}{2} \\
\end{gathered} \]
∴ Number of solution = 5

Question: Each of the angles β and γ that a given line makes with the positive y– and z–axes, respectively, is half of the angle that this line makes with the positive x-axes. Then the sum of all possible values of the angle β is
Solution:
\[\begin{gathered}
  \beta = \frac{\alpha }{2},\;\gamma = \frac{\alpha }{2} \\
  {\cos ^2}\alpha + {\cos ^2}\beta + {\cos ^2}\gamma = 1 \\
\end{gathered} \]
\[\begin{gathered}
  {\cos ^2}\alpha + 2{\cos ^2}\frac{\alpha }{2} = 1 \\
  {\cos ^2}\alpha + \cos \alpha = 0 \\
\end{gathered} \]
\[\begin{gathered}
  \cos \alpha \left( {\cos \alpha + 1} \right) = 0 \\
  \cos \alpha = 0, - 1 \\
  \alpha = \frac{\pi }{2},\pi \\
\end{gathered} \]
\[\begin{gathered}
  Now\;\beta = \frac{\alpha }{2} \Rightarrow \frac{\pi }{4},\;\frac{\pi }{2} \\
  so\;sum\;is\;\frac{{3\pi }}{4} \\
\end{gathered} \]

Question: If the probability that the random variable X takes the value x is given by P(X = x) = k(x + 1)3<sup>–x</sup>, x = 0,1,2,3......, where k is a constant, then P(X ≥ 3) is equal to
Solution:
\[\begin{gathered}
  \sum\limits_{x = 0}^\infty {k\left( {x + 1} \right){3^{ - x}}} = 1 \\
   \Rightarrow \;\frac{1}{k} = 1 + \frac{2}{3} + \frac{3}{{{3^2}}} + \frac{4}{{{3^3}}} + \;\;...\left( i \right) \\
\end{gathered} \]
\[\frac{1}{{3k}} = \frac{1}{3} + \frac{2}{{{3^2}}} + \frac{3}{{{3^3}}} + ...\;\;\;...\left( {ii} \right)\]
\[\begin{gathered}
  \left( i \right) - \left( {ii} \right) \Rightarrow \;\frac{1}{k} - \frac{1}{{3k}} = 1 + \frac{1}{3} + \frac{1}{{{3^2}}} + ... \\
   \Rightarrow \;k = \frac{4}{9} \\
\end{gathered} \]
\[\begin{gathered}
  P\left( {x \geqslant 3} \right) = 1 - P\left( {x = 0} \right) - P\left( {x = 1} \right) - P\left( {x = 2} \right) \\
   = 1 - k\left( {1 + \frac{2}{3} + \frac{3}{9}} \right) = \frac{1}{9} \\
\end{gathered} \]

Question: Let a1, a2, a3,… be a G. P. of increasing positive numbers. If a3a5 = 729 and a2 + a4 \[ = \frac{{111}}{4}\], then 24(a1 + a2 + a3) is equal to
Solution:
Let the Ist term of G.P. be a & common ratio be r
a3a5 = ar2.ar4 = 729
= a2r6 = 729
= ar3 = 27 ………………..(i)
a2 + a4 = ar + ar3\[ = \frac{{111}}{4}\]
\[ = {\text{ar = }}\frac{3}{4}............................\left( {{\text{ii}}} \right)\]
(i) ÷ (ii)
\[\frac{{{\text{a}}{{\text{r}}^3}}}{{{\text{ar}}}} = \frac{{27}}{{3/4}}\]
r2 = 36
r = 6 from (ii)
\[{\text{a}}\left( 6 \right) = \frac{3}{4} \Rightarrow {\text{a}} = \frac{1}{8}\]
Now, 24(a1 + a2 + a3)
= 24(a + ar + ar2) = 24a(1 + r + r2)
\[ = 24 \times \frac{1}{8}\left( {1 + 6 + 36} \right)\]
= 3(43)
= 129

= Total – [(All in R1 and R3) + (All in R2 and R3) + (All in R1 and R2)]

\[ = 5!\left( {56 - 1 - 1 - 6} \right) = 120\left( {48} \right)\]
\[ = 5760\]

Question:
If the system of equation
2x + \[\lambda \]y + 3z = 5
3x + 2y – z = 7
4x + 5y + \[\mu \]z = 9
has infinitely many solutions, then \[({\lambda ^2} + {\mu ^2})\] is equal to :
Solution:
\[\Delta = 0 \Rightarrow \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}}
  2&\lambda &3 \\
  3&2&{ - 1} \\
  4&5&\mu
\end{array}} \right| = 0\]
\[ \Rightarrow 2\left( {2\mu + 5} \right) + \lambda \left( { - 4 - 3\mu } \right) + 3\left( 7 \right) = 0\]
\[ \Rightarrow 4\mu - 3\lambda \mu - 4\lambda + 31 = 0\,\,\,\,\,\,.......(1)\]
\[{\Delta _3} = 0 \Rightarrow \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}}
  2&\lambda &5 \\
  3&2&7 \\
  4&5&9
\end{array}} \right| = 0\]
\[ \Rightarrow 2\left( { - 17} \right) + \lambda \left( 1 \right) + 5\left( 7 \right) = 0\]
\[ \Rightarrow \lambda = - 1\]
from equation (1)
\[4\mu + 3\mu + 4 + 31 = 0 \Rightarrow \boxed{\mu = - 5}\]
\[\therefore \boxed{{\lambda ^2} + {\mu ^2} = 26}\]

Question: Let \[\vec a = 2\hat i - 3\hat j + k,\vec b = 3\hat i + 2\hat j + 5k\] and a vector \[\vec c\] be such that\[\left( {\vec a - \vec c} \right) \times \vec b =  - 18\hat i - 3\hat j + 12k\] and \[\vec a.\vec c = 3\]. if \[\vec b \times \vec c = \vec d,\]then \[\left| {\vec a.\vec d} \right|\] is equal to :
Solution:
\[\vec a = 2\hat i - 3\hat j + \hat k,\vec b = 3\hat i + 2\hat j + 5\hat k\]
\[\vec a \times \vec b\left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}}
  i&j&k \\
  2&{ - 3}&1 \\
  3&2&5
\end{array}} \right|\]
\[ = - 17\hat i - 7\hat j + 13\hat k\]
\[\left( {\vec a - \vec c} \right) \times \vec b = - 18\hat i - 3\hat j + 12\hat k\]
\[ \Rightarrow \left( {\vec a \times \vec b} \right) - \left( {\vec c \times \vec b} \right) = - 18\hat i - 3\hat j + 12\hat k\]
\[ \Rightarrow \vec b \times \vec c = \left( { - 18\hat i - 3\hat j + 12\hat k} \right) - \left( {\vec a \times \vec b} \right)\]
\[ = \left( { - 18\hat i - 3\hat j + 12\hat k} \right) - \left( { - 17\hat i - 7\hat j + 13\hat k} \right)\]
\[\vec b \times \vec c = - \hat i + 4\hat j - \hat k\]
\[\therefore \vec a.\vec d = \vec a.\left( {\vec b \times \vec c} \right) = \left( {2i - 3j + k} \right).\left( { - \hat i + 4\hat j - \hat k} \right)\]
\[ = - 2 - 12 - 1 = - 15\]
\[\therefore \left| {\vec a.\vec d} \right| = 15\]

Question: The area of the region \[\left\{ {(x,\;y):\left| {x - y} \right| \leqslant y \leqslant 4\sqrt x } \right\}\]is
Solution:
\[\begin{gathered}
  \left| {x - y} \right| \leqslant y \leqslant 4\sqrt x \\
  Now\;y = \left| {x - y} \right| \\
\end{gathered} \]
\[\begin{gathered}
  {y^2} = {\left( {x - y} \right)^2} \\
   \Rightarrow \;y = \frac{x}{2}\;and\;x = 0 \\
\end{gathered} \]
\[Now\;area = \int\limits_0^{64} {\left( {4\sqrt x - \frac{x}{2}} \right)dx} \]
\[\begin{gathered}
   = \left[ {\frac{{4{x^{3/2}}}}{{3/2}} - \frac{{{x^2}}}{4}} \right]_0^{64} = \frac{8}{3}{.8^3} - \frac{{{{64}^2}}}{4} = {64^2}\left( {\frac{1}{{12}}} \right) \\
   = \frac{{1024}}{3} \\
\end{gathered} \]

Question: Let \[{\text{A = }}\left\{ { - 3, - 2, - 1,0,1,2,3} \right\}\]. Let R be a relation on A defined by xRy if and only if \[0 \leqslant {{\text{x}}^2} + 2{\text{y}} \leqslant 4\] . Let l be the number of elements in R and m be the minimum number of elements required to be added in R to make it a reflexive relation. then l + m is equal to

Let \[{\text{f}}\left( {\text{x}} \right) = \left\{ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}}
  {{{\left( {1 + {\text{ax}}} \right)}^{1/{\text{x}}}},} \\
  {1 + {\text{b,}}} \\
  {\frac{{{{\left( {{\text{x}} + 4} \right)}^{1/2}} - 2}}{{{{\left( {{\text{x}} + {\text{c}}} \right)}^{1/3}} - 2}},}
\end{array}\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,} \right.\begin{array}{*{20}{c}}
  {{\text{x}} < 0} \\
  {{\text{x}} = 0} \\
  {{\text{x}} > 0}
\end{array}\]be continuous at x = 0. Then ea bc is equal to
Solution:
\[{\text{f}}\left( {{0^ - }} \right) = {e^{\mathop {\lim }\limits_{{\text{x}} \to 0} \frac{{{\text{ax}}}}{{\text{x}}}}} = {{\text{e}}^{\text{a}}}\]
f(0) = 1 + b
\[{\text{f}}\left( {{0^ + }} \right) = \frac{{\frac{1}{{2\sqrt {{\text{x}} + 4} }}}}{{\frac{1}{2}{{\left( {{\text{x}} + {\text{c}}} \right)}^{ - \frac{2}{3}}}}} = \frac{{\frac{1}{{2\left( 2 \right)}}}}{{\frac{1}{3}.{{\text{c}}^{ - \frac{2}{3}}}}}\]
\[ = \frac{3}{4}{{\text{c}}^{2/3}}\]
Also at x = 0 ;
c1/3 = 2 ⇒ c = 8
So \[{\text{f}}\left( {{0^ + }} \right) = \frac{3}{4}{\left( 8 \right)^{2/3}} = 3\]
Now, ea = b + 1 = 3
ea.b.c = 3.2.8 = 48

Question: The integral \[\int\limits_0^\pi  {\frac{{8xdx}}{{4{{\cos }^2}x + {{\sin }^2}x}}} \]is equal to
Solution:
\[\begin{gathered}
  I = \int\limits_0^\pi {\frac{{8xdx}}{{4{{\cos }^2}x + {{\sin }^2}x}}} \\
  I = \int\limits_0^\pi {\frac{{8\left( {\pi - x} \right)dx}}{{4{{\cos }^2}x + {{\sin }^2}x}}} \\
\end{gathered} \]
\[\begin{gathered}
  2I = 8\pi \int\limits_0^\pi {\frac{{dx}}{{4{{\cos }^2}x + {{\sin }^2}x}}} \\
  2I = 8\pi \times 2\int\limits_0^{\pi /2} {\frac{{{{\sec }^2}x}}{{4 + {{\tan }^2}x}}dx} \\
\end{gathered} \]
\[I = 8\pi \int\limits_0^\infty {\frac{{dt}}{{4 + t}} = 8\pi \times \frac{1}{2}\left( {{{\tan }^{ - 1}}\frac{t}{2}} \right]_0^\infty } \]
\[ = 4\pi \times \frac{\pi }{2} = 2{\pi ^2}\]

Question: Let A be a matrix of order 3 × 3 and $\left| A \right| = 5$. If \[\left| {2{\text{adj}}\left( {3{\text{Aadj}}\left( {2{\text{A}}} \right)} \right)} \right| = {2^\alpha }{.3^\beta }{.5^\gamma }\alpha ,\beta ,\,\gamma \in \] N then α + β + γ is equal to
Solution:
\[\left| {2{\text{ adj}}\left( {3A{\text{ adj}}\left( {2{\text{A}}} \right)} \right)} \right|{\text{ }}\]
\[{2^3}.{\left| {3{\text{A adj}}\left( {2{\text{A}}} \right)} \right|^2}\]
\[{2^3}.{\left( {{3^3}} \right)^2}.{\left| {\text{A}} \right|^2}.{\left| {{\text{adj}}\left( {2{\text{A}}} \right)} \right|^2}\]
\[{2^3}{.3^6}.{\left| {\text{A}} \right|^2}.{\left( {{{\left| {{\text{2A}}} \right|}^2}} \right)^2}{\text{ }}\]
\[{2^3}{.3^6}.{\left| {\text{A}} \right|^2}{\left[ {{{\left( {{2^3}} \right)}^2}.{{\left| {\text{A}} \right|}^2}} \right]^2}{\text{ }}\]
\[{2^3}{.3^6}.{\left| {\text{A}} \right|^2}{.2^{12}}.{\left| {\text{A}} \right|^4}{\text{ }}\]
\[{2^{15}}{.3^6}.{\left| {\text{A}} \right|^6}{\text{ }}\]
\[{2^{15}}{.3^6}{.5^6} = {2^\alpha }{.3^\beta }{.5^\gamma }\]
\[\alpha = 15,\beta = 6,\,\gamma = 6\]
\[\alpha + \beta + \gamma = 27\]

Question:
Consider the following electrochemical cell at standard condition.
Au(s)|QH2, Q|NH4 X(0.01M)||Ag+(1M)|Ag(s) Ecell = +0.4V
The couple QH2/Q represents quinhydrone electrode, the half cell reaction is given below

$\left[ {{\text{Given}}:{\text{E}}_{{\text{A}}{{\text{g}}^ + }/{\text{Ag}}}^{\text{o}} = + 0.8{\text{Vand}}\frac{{2.303{\text{RT}}}}{{\text{F}}} = 0.06{\text{V}}} \right]$
The pKb value of the ammonium halide salt (NH4X) used here is ______. (nearest integer)
Solution:
${\text{Q}}{{\text{H}}_2} + 2{\text{A}}{{\text{g}}^ + } \to 2{\text{Ag}} + {\text{Q}} + 2{{\text{H}}^ + }$
${\text{E}} = {{\text{E}}^ \circ } - \frac{{0.06}}{2}\log {\left[ {{{\text{H}}^ + }} \right]^2}$
${\text{E}} = {{\text{E}}^ \circ } - 0.06 \times \log \left[ {{{\text{H}}^ + }} \right]$
${\text{pH}} = - \log \left( {{{\text{H}}^ + }} \right) = \frac{{{\text{E}} - {{\text{E}}^{\text{o}}}}}{{0.06}} = \frac{{0.4 - 0.1}}{{0.06}}$
$ = \frac{{0.3}}{{0.06}} = 5$
${\text{pH}} ({\text{N}}{{\text{H}}_4}{\text{X}}) = 7 - \frac{1}{2}{\text{p}}{{\text{K}}_{\text{b}}} - \frac{1}{2}\log {\text{C}}$
$5 = 7 - \frac{1}{2} \times {\text{p}}{{\text{K}}_{\text{b}}} - \frac{1}{2}\log ({10^{ - 2}})$
pKb = 6

Question: Let y = y (x) be the solution of the differential equation \[\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} + 2y{\sec ^2}x = 2{\sec ^2}x + 3\tan x.{\sec ^2}x\] such that \[y(0) = \frac{5}{4}.\] Then \[12\left( {y\left( {\frac{\pi }{4}} \right) - {e^{ - 2}}} \right)\] is equal to __________.
Solution:
\[I.F. = {e^{{\smallint ^{2\sec 2xdx}}}}\]
\[ = {e^{2\tan x}}\]
Solution of diff. eq.
\[y.{e^{2\tan x}} = \int {{e^{2\tan x}}\left( {2se{c^2}x + 3\tan x.{{\sec }^2}x} \right)dx} \]
\[y.{e^{2\tan x}} = \int {{e^{2\tan x}}.\left( {2{{\sec }^2}x} \right)dx + \int {{e^{2\tan x}}.\left( {3\tan x.{{\sec }^2}x} \right)} dx} \]
\[y.{e^{2tanx}} = {e^{2\tan x}}.2\tan x - \int {{e^{2\tan x}}.2{{\sec }^2}x \times 2\tan xdx + \int {{e^{2\tan x}}.3\tan x.{{\sec }^2}xdx} } \]
\[y.{e^{2\tan x}} = 2\tan x.{e^{2\tan x}} - \int {{e^{2\tan x}}.\tan x{{\sec }^2}xdx} \]
\[y.{e^{2\tan x}} = 2\tan x.{e^{2\tan x}} - \frac{{\tan x.{e^{2\tan x}}}}{2} + \frac{{{e^{2\tan x}}}}{4} + C\]
\[y = 2\tan x - \frac{{\tan x}}{2} + \frac{1}{4} + C{e^{ - 2\tan x}}\]
\[x = 0,y = \frac{5}{4}\]
\[c = 1\]
\[y\left( {\frac{\pi }{4}} \right) = \frac{7}{4} + {e^{ - 2}}\]
\[Then{\text{ }}12\left( {y\left( {\frac{\pi }{4}} \right) - {e^{ - 2}}} \right) = 12\left( {\frac{7}{4}} \right) = 21\]

Question: Let the point P of the focal chord PQ of the parabola y<sup>2</sup> = 16x be (1, –4). If the focus of the parabola divides the chord PQ in the ratio m : n, gcd(m, n) = 1, then m<sup>2</sup> + n<sup>2</sup> is equal to :
Solution:
y2 = 16x ; a = 4 focus S \[ \equiv \] (4, 0)

     2at1 = –4
\[ \Rightarrow 2\left( 4 \right){t_1} = - 4\]
\[ \Rightarrow {t_1} = - \frac{1}{2}\]
\[\because {t_1}{t_2} = - 1\]
\[ \Rightarrow {t_2} = 2\]
\[\therefore Q\left( {at_2^2,2a{t_2}} \right) = \left( {16,16} \right)\]
Let, S divides PQ internally in \[\lambda :1\] ratio
\[\therefore \frac{{16\lambda - 4}}{{\lambda + 1}} = 0\]
\[\lambda = \frac{1}{4} = \frac{m}{n}\]
\[\therefore {m^2} + {n^2} = 1 + 16 = 17\]

Question: Let \[\overrightarrow {\text{a}} = \widehat {\text{i}} + \widehat {\text{j}} + \hat {\text{k}} ,\overrightarrow {\text{b}} = 3\widehat {\text{i}} + 2\widehat {\text{j}} - \widehat {\text{k}},\overrightarrow {\text{c}} = \lambda \widehat {\text{j}} + \mu \widehat {\text{k}}\] and \[\widehat {\text{d}}\] be a unit vector such that \[\overrightarrow {\text{a}} \times \widehat {\text{d}} = \overrightarrow {\text{b}} \times \widehat {\text{d}}\] and \[\overrightarrow {\text{c}} .\widehat {\text{d}} = 1,\] If \[\overrightarrow {\text{c}} \] is perpendicular to \[\overrightarrow {\text{a}} \], then \[{\left| {3\lambda \widehat {\text{d}} + \mu \overrightarrow {\text{c}} } \right|^2}\] is equal to _______________.
Solution:
\[\overrightarrow {\text{a}} \times \overrightarrow {\text{d}} - \overrightarrow {\text{b}} \times \overrightarrow {\text{d}} = 0\]
\[\left( {\overrightarrow {\text{a}} - \overrightarrow {\text{b}} } \right) \times \overrightarrow {\text{d}} = 0\]
\[\overrightarrow {\text{d}} = {\text{t}}\left( {\overrightarrow {\text{a}} - \overrightarrow {\text{b}} } \right)\]
\[\overrightarrow {\text{d}} = {\text{t}}\left( { - 2\widehat {\text{i}} - \widehat {\text{j}} + 2\widehat {\text{k}}} \right)\]
\[\left| {\overrightarrow {\text{d}} } \right| = 1\]
\[\boxed{\left| {\text{t}} \right| = \frac{1}{3}}\]
\[\overrightarrow {\text{c}} .\overrightarrow {\,{\text{a}}} = 0\]
\[\lambda + \mu = 0\]
\[\mu = - \lambda \]
\[\overrightarrow {\text{c}} = \lambda \left( {\widehat {\text{j}} - \widehat {\text{k}}} \right),\,\,\,\,\,\,\boxed{{{\left| {\overrightarrow {\text{c}} } \right|}^2} = 2{\lambda ^2}}\]
\[\overrightarrow {\text{c}} .\widehat {\text{d}} = 1\]
t(–2, –1, 2) . λ (0, 1, –1) = 1
\[\lambda t = \frac{{ - 1}}{3} \Rightarrow \,\,\,\,\,\boxed{{\lambda ^2} = 1}\]
\[{\left| {3\lambda \widehat {\text{d}} + \mu \overrightarrow {\text{c}} } \right|^2} = 9{\lambda ^2}{\left| {\widehat {\text{d}}} \right|^2} + {\mu ^2}{\left| {\overrightarrow {\text{c}} } \right|^2} + 6\lambda \mu \left( {\widehat {\text{d}}.\overrightarrow {\text{c}} } \right)\]
\[ = 3{\lambda ^2} + 2{\lambda ^4}\]
\[ = 5\]

Solution:

Question: If \[\sum\limits_{r = 1}^9 {\left( {\frac{{r + 3}}{2^r}} \right)} {.\,^9}{C_r} = \alpha {\left( {\frac{3}{2}} \right)^9} - \beta ,\,\,\,\,\,\alpha ,\beta , \in N,{\text{ }}\] then (α + β )2 is equal to
Solution:
Given that \[\sum\limits_{r = 1}^9 {\left( {\frac{{r + 3}}{2}} \right)} {.^9}{C_r} = \alpha {\left( {\frac{3}{2}} \right)^9} - \beta ,\,\,\,\,\,\alpha ,\beta , \in N,{\text{ }}\]
Now, \[\sum\limits_{{\text{r}} = 1}^9 {\left( {\frac{{{\text{r}} + 3}}{{{2^{\text{r}}}}}} \right)} {.^{\text{9}}}{C_r} = \sum\limits_{{\text{r}} = 1}^9 {\left( {\frac{{\text{r}}}{{{2^{\text{r}}}}}} \right)} .9{\text{Cr}} + \sum\limits_{{\text{r}} = 1}^9 {\left( {\frac{3}{{{2^{\text{r}}}}}} \right)} {.^{\text{9}}}{C_{\text{r}}}\]
\[ = \sum\limits_{{\text{r}} = 1}^9 {\left( {\frac{9}{{{2^{\text{r}}}}}} \right)} {.^{\text{8}}}{C_{{\text{r}} - 1}} + 3\sum\limits_{{\text{r}} = 1}^9 {^9} {\text{Cr}}{\left( {\frac{1}{2}} \right)^{\text{r}}}\left[ {{\text{Using}}\,\,\,\frac{{^9{{\text{C}}_{\text{r}}}}}{{^8{C_{{\text{r}} - 1}}}} = \frac{9}{{\text{r}}}} \right]\]
\[ = \frac{9}{2}\sum\limits_{{\text{r}} = 1}^9 {^8{C_{{\text{r}} - 1}}} {\left( {\frac{1}{2}} \right)^{{\text{r}} - 1}} + 3\left( {\sum\limits_{{\text{r}} = 0}^9 {\left( {^9{{\text{C}}_r}{{\left( {\frac{1}{2}} \right)}^{\text{r}}}} \right) - 1} } \right)\]
\[ = \frac{9}{2}{\left( {1 + \frac{1}{2}} \right)^8} + 3\left( {{{\left( {1 + \frac{1}{2}} \right)}^9} - 1} \right)\]
\[ = \frac{9}{2}.{\left( {\frac{3}{2}} \right)^8} + {\left( {\frac{3}{2}} \right)^9} - 3 = 6.{\left( {\frac{3}{2}} \right)^9} - 3\]
Hence, α = 6, β = 3
Thus (α + β )2 = 81

Question: Let \[{\text{f}}\left( {\text{x}} \right) = \int {{{\text{x}}^3}} \sqrt {3 - {{\text{x}}^2}} {\text{dx}}{\text{. If 5f }}\left( {\sqrt 2 } \right) = - \,4\]then ƒ(1) is equal to
Solution:
Let 3 – x2 = t2
+x dx = –t dt
\[{\text{f}}\left( {\text{x}} \right) = \int {\left( {3 - {{\text{t}}^2}} \right)} .{\text{t}}\left( { - {\text{t dt}}} \right) + {\text{c}}\]
\[{\text{ = }}\int {\left( {{{\text{t}}^4} - 3{{\text{t}}^2}} \right)} {\text{ dt}} + c\]
\[ = \frac{{{{\text{t}}^5}}}{5} - {{\text{t}}^3} + {\text{c}}\]
\[{\text{f}}\left( {\text{x}} \right) = \frac{{\left( {3 - {{\text{x}}^2}} \right)}}{5} - {\left( {3 - {{\text{x}}^2}} \right)^{3/2}} + {\text{c}}\]
\[{\text{f}}\left( {\sqrt 2 } \right) = \frac{1}{5} - 1 + {\text{c}} = - \frac{4}{5}\]
\[\boxed{{\text{c = 0}}}\]
\[{\text{f}}\left( 1 \right) = \frac{{{2^{5/2}}}}{5} - {2^{3/2}}\]
\[ = {2^{1/2}}\left( {\frac{4}{5} - 2} \right)\]
\[\boxed{{\text{f}}\left( 1 \right) - \frac{{ - 6\sqrt 2 }}{5}}\]

Question: Let the product of the focal distances of the point \[{\text{P}}\left( {4,2\sqrt 3 } \right)\]on the hyperbola \[{\text{H}}:\frac{{{{\text{x}}^2}}}{{{{\text{a}}^2}}} - \frac{{{{\text{y}}^2}}}{{{{\text{b}}^2}}} = 1\]be 32. Let the length of the conjugate axis of H be p and the length of its latus rectum be q. Then p2 + q2 is equal to ……
Solution:
\[\frac{{{{\text{x}}^2}}}{{{{\text{a}}^2}}} - \frac{{{{\text{y}}^2}}}{{{{\text{b}}^2}}} = 1\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,.......\left( 1 \right)\]
\[{\text{P}}\left( {4,2\sqrt 3 } \right)\]
PS1.PS2 = 32
|PS1 – PS2| = 2a
\[{\text{P}}\left( {4,2\sqrt 3 } \right)\]lies on H
\[\therefore \frac{{16}}{{{{\text{a}}^2}}} - \frac{{12}}{{{{\text{b}}^2}}} = 1\]
16b2 – 12a2 = a2b2 …………....(2)
|PS1 – PS2|2 = 4a2
\[{\text{PS}}_1^2 + {\text{PS}}_2^2 - 2{\text{P}}{{\text{S}}_1}.{\text{P}}{{\text{S}}_2} = 4{{\text{a}}^2}\]
(ae – 4)2 + 12 + (ae + 4)2 + 12 – 64 = 4a2
2a2e2 – 8 = 4a2
a2 + b2 – 4 = 2a2
b2 – a2 = 4
(2) & (3) ⇒ 16(a2 + 4) – 12a2 = a2(a2 + 4)
⇒ 16a2 + 64 – 12a2 = a4 + 4a2
⇒ a4 = 64
⇒ a2 = 8
∴ b2 = 12
\[{{\text{p}}^2} + {{\text{q}}^2} = 4{{\text{b}}^2} + \frac{{4{{\text{b}}^2}}}{{{{\text{a}}^2}}}\]
= 120

Question: Let f be a function such that \[f(x) + 3f\left( {\frac{{24}}{x}} \right)\]= 4x, x ≠ 0. Then f(3) + f(8) is equal to

'x' g of NaCl is added to water in a beaker with a lid. The temperature of the system is raised from 1°C to 25°C. Which out of the following plots, is best suited for the change in the molarity (M) of the solution with respect to temperature ?
[Consider the solubility of NaCl remains unchanged over the temperature range]
Solution:
\[\mathop {Water}\limits_{{1^o}C} \xrightarrow[{decreases}]{{Volume}}\mathop {Water}\limits_{{4^o}C} \xrightarrow[{thermal{\text{ expansion}}}]{{Volume{\text{ increases}}}}\mathop {Water}\limits_{{{25}^o}C} \]

Hence

Question: The sum 1 + 3 + 11 + 25 + 45 + 71 + … upto 20 terms, is equal to
Solution:
Given sum is
Sn = 1 + 3 + 11 + 25 + 45 + 71 + ... + Tn
First order differences are in A.P.
Thus, we can assume that
Tn = an2 + bn + c
\[{\text{Solving }}\left\{ \begin{gathered}
  {\text{ }}{{\text{T}}_{\text{1}}}{\text{ = 1 = a + b + c}} \\
  {{\text{T}}_{\text{2}}}{\text{ = 3 = 4a + 2b + c}} \\
  {{\text{T}}_{\text{3}}}{\text{ = 11 = 9a + 3b + c}} \\
\end{gathered} \right\},\]
we get a = 3, b = –7, c = 5
Hence, general term of given series is
Tn = 3n2 – 7n + 5
Hence, required sum equals
\[\sum\limits_{{\text{n}} = 1}^{{\text{n}} = 20} {\left( {3{{\text{n}}^{\text{2}}} - 7n + 5} \right) = 3\left( {\frac{{20.21.41}}{6}} \right) - 7\left( {\frac{{20.21}}{2}} \right) + 5\left( {20} \right) = 7240} \]

Tags.Topic: Sum of series by difference method

Question: Let A = {–2,–1,0, 1, 2, 3}. let R be a relation on A defined by xRy if and only if y = max{x, 1}. Let l be the number of elements in R. Let m and n be the minimum number of elements required to be added in R to make it reflexive and symmetric relations, respectively. Then l + m + n is equal to

A sinusoidal wave of wavelength 7.5 cm travels a distance of 1.2 cm along the x-direction in 0.3 sec. The crest P is at x = 0 at t = 0 sec and maximum displacement of the wave is 2 cm. Which equation correctly represents this wave ?
Solution:
\[v = \frac{{dis\tan ce}}{{time}}\]
\[v = \frac{{1.2}}{{0.3}} = 4cm/s\]
\[k = \frac{{2\pi }}{\lambda } = \frac{{2\pi }}{{7.5}} = \frac{{4\pi }}{{15}} = 0.83\]
\[v = \frac{\omega }{k} \Rightarrow \omega = vk = 4 \times \frac{{4\pi }}{{15}} = 3.35\]
\[So{\text{ y}}\;{\text{ = A cos}}\left( {kx - \omega t} \right)\]

Question: If the measured angular separation between the second minimum to the left of the central maximum and the third minimum to the right of the central maximum is 30° in a single slit diffraction pattern recorded using 628 nm light, then the width of the slit is ____ μ m.
Solution:
\[{\theta _1} = {\sin ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{2\lambda }}{a}} \right)\]
\[{\theta _2} = {\sin ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{3\lambda }}{a}} \right)\]
\[\because {\theta _1} + {\theta _2} = {30^o}\]
\[ \Rightarrow {\sin ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{2\lambda }}{a}} \right) + {\sin ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{3\lambda }}{a}} \right) = \frac{\pi }{6}\]
Here λ = 628 nm
After solving
A = 6.07 μ m
Approximate Method :
θ = θ 1 + θ 2
\[ \Rightarrow \frac{\pi }{6} = \frac{{2\lambda }}{a} + \frac{{3\lambda }}{a}\]
\[ \Rightarrow \frac{\pi }{6} = \frac{5}{a}(628\,\,nm)\]
\[ \Rightarrow a = 6\,\mu m\]

It is brought near to an infinitely long non conducting sheet of charge density 'σ ' as shown in figure. If string subtends an angle of 45° with the sheet at equilibrium the charge density of sheet will be :
(Given, \[{\varepsilon _0} = 8.85 \times {10^{ - 12}}\frac{F}{m}\] and acceleration due to gravity, g = 10 m/s2)
Solution:
qE = mg
\[q\left[ {\frac{\sigma }{{2{\varepsilon _0}}}} \right] = mg\]
\[\sigma = \frac{{2 \times 8.85 \times {{10}^{ - 12}} \times 100 \times {{10}^{ - 6}} \times 10}}{{10 \times {{10}^{ - 6}}}}\]

Question: A person travelling on a straight line moves with a uniform velocity v<sub>1</sub> for a distance x and with a uniform velocity v<sub>2</sub> for the next  \[\frac{3}{2}x\] distance. A small mirror of mass m is suspended by a massless thread of length l. Then the small angle through which the thread will be deflected when a short pulse of laser of energy E falls normal on the mirror
(c = speed of light in vacuum and g = acceleration due to gravity)
Solution:
Force due to beam assuming complete reflection
\[F = \frac{{2P}}{C} = \frac{2}{C}\frac{{dE}}{{dt}};P\,is\,power\]
So change in momentum of mirror.
\[m(V - 0) = \int {Fdt} = \frac{2}{C}\int {dE} = \frac{{2E}}{C}\]
Now using work energy theorem ……(1)

\[ - mg\ell (1 - \cos \theta ) = 0 - \frac{1}{2}m{v^2}\]
\[g\ell \left( {2{{\sin }^2}\frac{\theta }{2}} \right) = \frac{{{v^2}}}{2}\]
as θ is small
\[g\ell 2{\left( {\frac{\theta }{2}} \right)^2} = \frac{1}{2}\frac{{4{E^2}}}{{{m^2}{c^2}}}\,\,\,\,\,\,({\text{from}}\,\,{\text{eq}}.\,\,(1))\]
\[g\ell {\theta ^2} = \frac{{4{E^2}}}{{{m^2}{c^2}}}\]
\[\theta = \frac{{2E}}{{mc\sqrt {g\ell } }}\]

Question: A motor operating on 100 V draws a current of 1 A. If the efficiency of the motor is 91.6%, then the loss of power in units of cal/s is
Solution:
\[{P_{input}} = Vi = 100\;W\]
\[\begin{gathered}
  \eta = \frac{{{P_{out}}}}{{{P_{input}}}} = 0.916 \\
  {P_{out}} = 91.6\;W \\
\end{gathered} \]
Loss = 100 – 91.6 = 8.4 J/s = 2 cal/s

Question: Conductor wire ABCDE with each arm 10 cm in length is placed in magnetic field of \[\frac{1}{{\sqrt 2 }}\] Tesla, perpendicular to its plane. When conductor is pulled towards right with constant velocity of 10 cm/s, induced emf between points A and E is ___________ mV.
Solution:
As field is uniform we can replace the bent wire with straight wire from A to B.
So EMF :
\[\varepsilon = Bv{\ell _{AB}}\]
\[ = \frac{1}{{\sqrt 2 }} \times \frac{{10cm}}{5} \times 2(10\,\sin \,{45^o})cm\]
ε = 10 mV

Question: Consider the sound wave travelling in ideal gases of He, CH<sub>4</sub>, and CO<sub>2</sub>. The ratio of the speed of sound through the gases \[{v _{He}}:{v_{C{H_4}}}:{v_{C{O_2}}}\]is given by

Question: For the determination of refractive index of glass slab, a travelling microscope is used whose main scale contains 300 equal divisions equals to 15 cm. The vernier scale attached to the microscope has 25 divisions equals to 24 divisions of main scale. The least count (LC) of the travelling microscope is (in cm) :

The displacement x versus time graph is shown below.

(A) The average velocity during 0 to 3 s is 10 m/s
(B) The average velocity during 3 to 5 s is 0 m/s
(C) The instantaneous velocity at t = 2 s is 5 m/s
(D) The average velocity during 5 to 7 s and instantaneous velocity at t = 6.5 s are equal
(E) The average velocity from t = 0 to t = 9 s is zero
Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
Solution:
\[\left\langle {\overrightarrow v } \right\rangle = \frac{{\Delta \overrightarrow s }}{{\Delta t}} = \frac{{{S_f} - {S_i}}}{{{t_f} - {t_i}}}\]
\[\overrightarrow v = \frac{{ds}}{{dt}} = slope\]
\[\left( {\text{A}} \right)\,0\,{\text{to}}\,3\,\sec ; < \overrightarrow v > = \frac{{5 - 0}}{3} = 5/3\,m/s\]
\[\left( {\text{B}} \right)\,0\,{\text{to}}\,5\,\sec ; < \overrightarrow v > = \frac{{5 - 5}}{2} = 0\]
\[\left( {\text{C}} \right)\,t = 2;\,{\text{slope}} = \overrightarrow v = 5\,m/s\]
\[\left( {\text{D}} \right)\,t = 5\,to\,7\,\sec ;\, < \overrightarrow v > = \frac{{0 - 5}}{2} = - 2.5\,m/s\]
At t = 6.5 sec; \[\overrightarrow v = 10\]
\[\left( {\text{E}} \right)\,t = 0\,{\text{to}}\,t = 9;\, < \overrightarrow v > = 0\]

Question:

\[{P_P} + {k_P} = {P_{\text{o}}} + {k_0}\]
\[ - \frac{{GMm}}{{4R}} + \frac{1}{2}mV_P^2 = 0\]
\[{V_P} = \sqrt {\frac{{GM}}{{2R}}} \]

Question: Consider a rectangular sheet of solid material of length \[\ell \]= 9 cm and width d = 4 cm. The coefficient of linear expansion is \[\alpha \] = 3.1 × 10<sup>–5</sup> K<sup>–1</sup> at room temperature and one atmospheric pressure. The mass of sheet m = 0.1 kg and the specific heat capacity C<sub>v</sub> = 900 J kg<sup>–1</sup>K<sup>–1</sup>. If the amount of heat supplied to the material is 8.1 × 10<sup>2</sup> J then change in area of the rectangular sheet is :-
Solution:
\[\Delta Q = ms\Delta T\]
\[8.1 \times {10^2} = 0.1 \times 900 \times \Delta T\]
\[\Delta A = {A_0}2\alpha \,\Delta T = 2.0 \times {10^{ - 6}}{m^2}\]

Question: A body of mass m is suspended by two strings making angles θ<sub>1</sub> and θ<sub>2</sub> with the horizontal ceiling with tensions T<sub>1</sub> and T<sub>2</sub> simultaneously. T<sub>1</sub> and T<sub>2</sub> are related by T<sub>1</sub> =  \[\sqrt 3 {T_2}\]. the angle θ <sub>1</sub> = \[{\theta _2}\] are

Question: A cylindrical rod of length 1 m and radius 4 cm is mounted vertically. It is subjected to a shear force of 10<sup>5</sup> N at the top. Considering infinitesimally small displacement in the upper edge, the angular displacement \[\theta \] of the rod axis from its original position would be : (shear moduli, G = 10<sup>10</sup> N/m<sup>2</sup>)
Solution:
Shear moduli = \[\frac{{{\sigma _{{\text{shear}}}}}}{\theta }\]
\[{10^{10}} = \frac{{{{10}^5}}}{{\pi \times 16 \times {{10}^{ - 4}}}} \times \frac{1}{\theta }\]
\[\theta = \frac{1}{{160\pi }}{\text{Radian}}\]

Question: From the combination of resistors with resistance values \[{R_1} = {R_2} = {R_3} = 5\Omega \] and \[{R_4} = 10\Omega \], which of the following combination is the best circuit to get an equivalent resistance of \[6\Omega \] ?

Question: Two slabs with square cross section of different materials (1, 2) with equal sides (l) and thickness d<sub>1</sub> and d<sub>2</sub> such that d<sub>2</sub> = 2d<sub>1</sub> and l > d<sub>2</sub>. Considering lower edges of these slabs are fixed to the floor, we apply equal shearing force on the narrow faces. The angle of deformation is θ <sub>2</sub> = 2θ <sub>1</sub>. If the shear moduli of material 1 is 4 × 10<sup>9</sup> N/m<sup>2</sup>, then shear moduli of material 2 is x ×10<sup>9</sup> N/m<sup>2</sup>, where value of x is ____________.
Solution:
Deformation angle
\[2{\theta _1} = {\theta _2}\]
\[ \Rightarrow 2\frac{{{\sigma _1}}}{{{\eta _1}}} = \frac{{{\sigma _2}}}{{{\eta _2}}}\]

\[ \Rightarrow 2\left( {\frac{F}{{\ell {d_1}{\eta _1}}}} \right) = \frac{F}{{\ell {d_2}{\eta _2}}}\]
\[ \Rightarrow {\eta _2} = \frac{{{\eta _1}}}{4} = 1 \times {10^9} \Rightarrow x = 1\]

Question: In an electromagnetic system, the quantity representing the ratio of electric flux and magnetic flux has dimension of M<sup>P</sup>L<sup>Q</sup>T<sup>R</sup>A<sup>S</sup>, where value of ‘Q’ and ‘R’ are
When an object is placed 40 cm away from a spherical mirror an image of magnification \[\frac{1}{2}\] is produced. To obtain an image with magnification of \[\frac{1}{3}\], the object is to be moved.

A physical quantity C is related to four other quantities p, q, r and s as follows
\[C = \frac{{p{q^2}}}{{{r^3}\sqrt s }}\]
The percentage errors in the measurement of p, q, r and s are 1%, 2% 3% and 2% respectively. The percentage error in the measurement of C will be _________%.
Solution:
\[\begin{gathered}
  C = {P^1}{q^2}{r^{ - 3}}{s^{1/2}} \\
  {\left( {\frac{{dC}}{C}} \right)_{\max }} = \frac{{dP}}{P} + \frac{{2dq}}{q} + \frac{{3dr}}{r} + \frac{1}{2}\;\frac{{ds}}{s} \\
\end{gathered} \]
\[ = \left( {1 + 2 \times 2 + 3 \times 3 + \frac{1}{2} \times 2} \right)\% \]
= 15 %

Question: The excess pressure inside a soap bubble A in air is half the excess pressure inside another soap bubble B in air. If the volume of the bubble A is n times the volume of the bubble B, then, the value of n is _______.

  \frac{{{R_A}}}{{{R_B}}} = \frac{{\Delta {P_B}}}{{\Delta {P_A}}} = 2 \\
  \frac{{{V_A}}}{{{V_B}}} = {\left( {\frac{{{R_A}}}{{{R_B}}}} \right)^3} = 8 \\
\end{gathered} \]

Question: Two cells of emf 1V and 2V and internal resistance 2 Ω and 1 Ω, respectively, are connected in series with an external resistance of 6 Ω. The total current in the circuit is I<sub>1</sub>. Now the same two cells in parallel configuration are connected to same external resistance. In this case, the total current drawn is I<sub>2</sub>. The value of \[\left( {\frac{{{I_1}}}{{{I_2}}}} \right)\;is\;\frac{x}{3}\]. The value of x is ______.

\[\begin{gathered}
  {\varepsilon _{eq}} = 3 \\

  {i_1} = \frac{3}{9} = \frac{1}{3} \\
\end{gathered} \]

\[{\varepsilon _{eq}} = \frac{{\frac{{{\varepsilon _1}}}{{{r_1}}} + \frac{{{\varepsilon _2}}}{{{r_2}}}}}{{\frac{1}{{{r_1}}} + \frac{1}{{{r_2}}}}}\]

\[\begin{gathered}

Question: A circular ring and a solid sphere having same radius roll down on an inclined plane from rest without slipping. The ratio of their velocities when reached at the bottom of the plane is \[\sqrt {\frac{x}{5}} \]where x = ____________.(Nearest integer)
Solution:
Applying Mechanical Energy conservation :
\[ \Rightarrow {k_i} + {U_i} = {k_f} + {U_f}\]
\[ \Rightarrow 0 + Mgh = \frac{1}{2}m{v^2}\left( {1 + \frac{{{k^2}}}{{{R^2}}}} \right) + 0\]
\[ \Rightarrow V = \sqrt {\frac{{2gh}}{{1 + \frac{{{k^2}}}{{{R^2}}}}}} \]
So Ratio of velocities
\[\frac{{{V_{Ring}}}}{{{V_{solids\,\,sphere}}}} = \sqrt {\frac{{1 + \frac{2}{5}}}{{1 + 1}}} = \sqrt {\frac{7}{{10}}} \]
x = 3.5 Rounding off x = 4

Question: Two simple pendulums having lengths l<sub>1</sub> and l<sub>2</sub> with negligible string mass undergo angular displacements θ <sub>1</sub> and θ <sub>2</sub>, from their mean positions, respectively. \[ \Rightarrow {\theta _1}{\ell _2} = {\theta _2}{\ell _2}\]

Question: There are two vessels filled with an ideal gas where volume of one is double the volume of other. The large vessel contains the gas at 8 kPa at 1000 K while the smaller vessel contains the gas at 7 kPa at 500 K. If the vessels are connected to each other by a thin tube allowing the gas to flow and the temperature of both vessels is maintained at 600 K, at steady state the pressure in the vessels will be (in kPa).
Solution:
Number of masses will remain constant
\[{n_1} + {n_2} = {n_f}\]
\[\frac{{{P_1}{V_1}}}{{R{T_1}}} + \frac{{{P_2}{V_2}}}{{R{T_2}}} = \frac{{{P_f}{V_f}}}{{R{T_f}}}\]
\[\frac{{8 \times 2V}}{{R \times 1000}} + \frac{{7 \times V}}{{R \times 500}} = \frac{{{P_f}\left( {3V} \right)}}{{R \times 600}}\]
\[\frac{{30}}{{1000}} = \frac{{{P_f}}}{{200}}\]
\[{P_f} = 6kPa\]

Assertion wrong
at ∞ U = 0
∴ Reason correct.

Question: Three parallel plate capacitors C1, C2 and C3 each of capacitance 5 µF are connected as shown in figure. The effective capacitance between points A and B, when the space between the parallel plates of C1 capacitor is filled with a dielectric medium having dielectric constant of 4, is:
Solution:
After dielectric
\[{C_1} = 4C\]
\[{C_1} = 4 \times 5 = 20\mu F\]
\[{C_2} = {C_3} = 5\mu F\]
C1 & C2 are in series which is parallel to C3 So
\[{C_{eq}} = \frac{{{C_1}{C_2}}}{{{C_1} + {C_2}}} + {C_3} \Rightarrow \frac{{20 \times 5}}{{20 + 5}} + 5\]
\[ = 4 + 5 = 9\mu F\]
Correct Option (c)

Question: A particle of charge q, mass m and kinetic energy E enters in magnetic field perpendicular to its velocity and undergoes a circular arc of radius(r). Which of the following curves represents the variation of r with E?
Solution:
\[\frac{{m{v^2}}}{r} = qvB\]
\[mv = qBr\]
\[E = \frac{1}{2}m{v^2}\]
\[E = \frac{1}{2}m\left( {\frac{{{q^2}{B^2}{r^2}}}{{{m^2}}}} \right) = \frac{{{q^2}{B^2}{r^2}}}{{2m}}\]
\[E = \left( {\frac{{{q^2}{B^2}}}{{2m}}} \right){r^2}\]
\[{r^2} \propto E\]

Question: A sample of a liquid is kept at 1 atm. It is compressed to 5 atm which leads to change of volume of 0.8 cm<sup>3</sup>. If the bulk modulus of the liquid is 2 GPa, the initial volume of the liquid was ______ litre. (Take 1 atm = 10<sup>5</sup> Pa)
Solution:
Given, Initial pressure of liquid (Pi) = 1 atm Final pressure of liquid (Pf) = 5 atm
Change in pressure (dP) = Pf – Pi = 4 atm = 4 × 105 Pa
Change in volume (dV) = – 0.8 cm3
Bulk modulus (B) = 2 × 109 Pa
Now, ${\text{B}} = \frac{{ - {\text{dP}}}}{{({\text{dV}}/{\text{V}})}} \Rightarrow {\text{V}} = - {\text{B}}\left( {\frac{{{\text{dV}}}}{{{\text{dP}}}}} \right)$
$ \Rightarrow {\text{V}} = - 2 \times {10^9} \times \frac{{( - 0.8 \times {{10}^{ - 6}})}}{{4 \times {{10}^5}}}$
$ = 4 \times {10^{ - 3}}{{\text{m}}^3} = 4\,\,{\text{litre}}$

Question: Two polarisers P<sub>1</sub> and P<sub>2</sub> are placed in such a way that the intensity of the transmitted light will be zero. A third polariser P<sub>3</sub> is inserted in between P<sub>1</sub> and P<sub>2</sub>, at the particular angle between P<sub>2</sub> and P<sub>3</sub>. The transmitted intensity of the light passing the through all three polarisers is maximum. The angle between the polarisers P<sub>2</sub> and P<sub>3</sub> is :
Solution:
Through P2 \[{I_1} = {I_0}{\sin ^2}\left( {\frac{\pi }{2} - \theta } \right)\]

\[{I_1} = {I_0}{\cos ^2}\theta \]
Through \[{P_3}\,{I_{net}} = \left( {{I_0}{{\cos }^2}\theta } \right){\sin ^2}\theta \]
\[{I_{net}} = \frac{{{I_0}}}{4}{\left[ {\sin \left( {2\theta } \right)} \right]^2}\,{\text{for}}\,\max {I_{net}}\,\theta = 45^\circ \]
So angle between P2 and P3 = \[\frac{\pi }{4}\]

A light ray is incident on the flat face of the block with incident angle θ as shown in figure. What is the maximum value of θ for which light suffers total internal reflection at the top surface of the block ?

r + θ C = 90°
µ1sinθ = µ2sinr
\[\begin{gathered}
  \sin \theta = \frac{{{\mu _2}}}{{{\mu _1}}}\sin \left( {90 - {\theta _c}} \right) \\
  \sin \theta = \frac{{{\mu _2}}}{{{\mu _1}}}\cos {\theta _c} \\
  \sin {\theta _c} = \frac{{{\mu _1}}}{{{\mu _2}}} \\
\end{gathered} \]
\[\begin{gathered}
  \sin \theta = \frac{{{\mu _2}}}{{{\mu _1}}}\sqrt {1 - \frac{{\mu _1^2}}{{\mu _2^2}}} \\

\[\begin{gathered}

(m : mass of the electron, h : Planck’s constant and c : speed of light)
Solution:
K.E = E – W
\[{\lambda _e} = \frac{h}{{\sqrt {2mK.E} }},\;E = \frac{{hc}}{{{\lambda _i}}},\;W = \frac{{hc}}{{{\lambda _0}}}\]
\[\begin{gathered}

An infinitely long wire has uniform linear charge density λ = 2 nC/m. The net flux through a Gaussian cube of side length \[\sqrt 3 \]cm, if the wire passes through any two corners of the cube, that are maximally displaced from each other, would be xNm2C–1, where x is :

Question:

\[\begin{gathered}
  u = 360 \times \frac{5}{{18}} = 100\;m/s \\
  x = u \times t = 2 \times {10^3}m \\
\end{gathered} \]
\[\begin{gathered}
  t = \sqrt {\frac{{2H}}{g}} \Rightarrow H = \frac{{{t^2}g}}{2} \\
  H = \frac{{400 \times 10}}{2} \\
\end{gathered} \]
H = 2000 m
\[\begin{gathered}
  D = \sqrt {{x^2} + {H^2}} \\
  D = 2\sqrt 2 km \\
\end{gathered} \]

Question:

Solution:
All bodies have same mass and same radius.
A \[ \to \] Disc

and, \[I = \frac{{M{R^2}}}{4}\]
